title: Process mining framework for software processes
authors: V. Rubin; C. Günther; Wil M.P. van der Aalst; E. Kindler; B. V. Dongen; Wilhelm Schäfer
venue: International Conference on the Software Process
research_question: Como extrair e analisar modelos de processos de software a partir de informações armazenadas em sistemas de gerenciamento de configuração de software (SCM)?
main_finding: O framework ProM pode ser utilizado para extrair modelos de processos de software a partir de dados de SCM, permitindo que engenheiros identifiquem, entendam, analisem e otimizem seus processos de desenvolvimento através de process mining em múltiplas perspectivas (controle, informação e organização).
limitations: não_mencionado

title: Using process mining metrics to measure noisy process fidelity
authors: C. Thomson; M. Gheorghe
venue: International Conference on Evaluation & Assessment in Software Engineering
abstract: Background: When teams follow a software development process they do not follow the process consistently. We need a method to measure their fidelity to that process. Objective: To evaluate Rozinat and Aalst's metrics for process conformance to a state based model on noisy data (Rozinat and van der Aalst, 2008). Method: We instructed 14 teams that were developing a software system using Extreme Programming (XP) to record the events of their project (for example writing code, or testing). We calculated the values of the proposed metrics by comparing the data collected to a process model of XP. Results: 13 teams recoded data that we treat as a multiple case study. The fitness metric gave varying results over the teams that corresponded to the number of event types used in the correct order. The appropriateness metrics measured the same values for all teams. Conclusion: The fitness metric is useful for measuring fidelity, but the appropriateness metrics do not measure over fitting well with noisy data. In addition neither metric gave useful information about other aspects like iteration.
research_question: Métricas de conformance propostas para avaliar modelos de processos minerados são válidas para medir fidelidade a um processo desejado em dados reais e ruidosos de engenharia de software?
main_finding: A métrica de fitness é útil para medir fidelidade ao processo, mostrando variações que correspondem ao uso correto de tipos de eventos na ordem esperada. As métricas de apropriabilidade não se mostraram eficazes em detectar overfitting em dados ruidosos e não forneceram informações sobre outros aspectos como iteração.
limitations: O estudo foi conduzido com 14 equipes em contexto acadêmico desenvolvendo com Extreme Programming, o que pode limitar a generalização. As métricas avaliadas não capturam adequadamente aspectos importantes do processo além da conformance simples.

title: Discovering changes of the change control board process during a software development project using process mining
authors: Jana Samalikova; J. Trienekens; R. Kusters; J.Samalikova; A.J.M.M.Weijters; Nl
venue: European Conference on Software Process Improvement
research_question: Como o processo de Change Control Board (CCB) real desvia do processo documentado durante as diferentes fases do desenvolvimento de software e quais são as implicações dessas deviações para atividades de melhoria de processo?
main_finding: O estudo revelou que o processo CCB real diverge significativamente do processo documentado em 70% dos casos. A aplicação de process mining permitiu identificar atalhos e desvios causados por pressão de tempo e falta de disciplina durante diferentes fases do projeto, possibilitando propostas de melhoria de processo baseadas na prática real.
limitations: O estudo é específico para uma organização e um tipo particular de projeto de software (middleware embedded-software). Limitações sobre generalização dos resultados para outros contextos não são extensivamente discutidas.

title: Applicability of Process Discovery Algorithms for Software Organizations
authors: Burcu Akman; Onur Demirörs
venue: EUROMICRO Conference on Software Engineering and Advanced Applications
journal_name: 2009 35th Euromicro Conference on Software Engineering and Advanced Applications
research_question: Qual é a aplicabilidade de algoritmos de descoberta de processos para identificar processos as-is em organizações de software? Como os resultados desses algoritmos se comparam com os processos realmente executados?
main_finding: O estudo aplicou quatro algoritmos de descoberta de processos (RNet, Ktail, Markov Models e Heuristic Mining) em dados de processos de review de uma organização de software CMMI Nível 3. Os resultados foram comparados com processos realmente executados e definições escritas, evidenciando discrepâncias e fraquezas dos algoritmos, especialmente na modelagem de processos não estruturados.
limitations: Os algoritmos de process mining não são muito bem-sucedidos com processos não estruturados, gerando modelos tipo spaghetti e demasiadamente detalhados. A extração de dados foi realizada apenas do processo de review utilizando ferramentas de controle de configuração.

title: Toward objective software process information: Experiences from a case study
authors: Jana Samalikova; R. Kusters; J. Trienekens; Ton Weijters; Paul Siemons
venue: Software quality journal
journal_name: Software Quality Journal
abstract: A critical problem in software development is the monitoring, control and improvement in the processes of software developers. Software processes are often not explicitly modeled, and manuals to support the development work contain abstract guidelines and procedures. Consequently, there are huge differences between ‘actual’ and ‘official’ processes: “the actual process is what you do, with all its omissions, mistakes, and oversights. The official process is what the book, i.e., a quality manual, says you are supposed to do” (Humphrey in A discipline for software engineering. Addison-Wesley, New York, 1995). Software developers lack support to identify, analyze and better understand their processes. Consequently, process improvements are often not based on an in-depth understanding of the ‘actual’ processes, but on organization-wide improvement programs or ad hoc initiatives of individual developers. In this paper, we show that, based on particular data from software development projects, the underlying software development processes can be extracted and that automatically more realistic process models can be constructed. This is called software process mining (Rubin et al. in Process mining framework for software processes. Software process dynamics and agility. Springer Berlin, Heidelberg, 2007). The goal of process mining is to better understand the development processes, to compare constructed process models with the ‘official’ guidelines and procedures in quality manuals and, subsequently, to improve development processes. This paper reports on process mining case studies in a large industrial company in The Netherlands. The subject of the process mining is a particular process: the change control board (CCB) process. The results of process mining are fed back to practice in order to subsequently improve the CCB process.
research_question: Como extrair e modelar automaticamente processos reais de desenvolvimento de software a partir de dados de sistemas de configuração, e como esses modelos se comparam com os processos oficiais documentados?
main_finding: O artigo demonstra que process mining pode ser aplicado com sucesso a processos de software rotineiros e estruturados, particularmente ao process Change Control Board (CCB), extraindo modelos de processo reais a partir de dados de SCM. Os modelos descobertos revelam diferenças significativas entre os processos 'oficiais' documentados e os processos 'reais' executados, fornecendo insights objetivos para melhorias de processo.
limitations: O estudo foca em processos rotineiros e estruturados de software, limitando a aplicabilidade a atividades criativas menos estruturadas. A qualidade dos resultados de process mining depende fortemente da preparação adequada dos dados.

title: Unsupervised discovery of intentional process models from event logs
authors: G. Khodabandelou; Charlotte Hug; R. Deneckère; C. Salinesi
venue: IEEE Working Conference on Mining Software Repositories
research_question: Como descobrir automaticamente modelos de processos intencionais a partir de logs de eventos de atividades de usuários em plataformas de software?
main_finding: O MapMinerMethod (MMM) automatiza a construção de modelos intencionais a partir de logs de eventos usando Hidden Markov Models para modelar estratégias de usuários. O método infere intenções e constrói modelos intencionais em granularidades finas e grosseiras usando a notação Map, gerando modelos que oferecem nova compreensão de processos de software e podem ser utilizados em sistemas de recomendação.
limitations: não_mencionado

title: Applied process mining in software development
authors: Maria Laura Sebu; H. Ciocarlie
venue: International Symposium on Applied Computational Intelligence and Informatics
journal_name: 2014 IEEE 9th IEEE International Symposium on Applied Computational Intelligence and Informatics (SACI)
research_question: Qual é o modelo de processo real em um desenvolvimento de software terceirizado e quais são as diferenças entre o modelo teórico e o modelo real? Quais melhorias podem ser identificadas no processo para evitar retrabalho?
main_finding: O estudo aplicou técnicas de process mining para extrair o modelo real de processo de desenvolvimento de software de uma empresa automotiva, identificando desvios do modelo teórico inicial. O framework ProM foi utilizado para descobrir o processo, analisar a rede organizacional e extrair informações estatísticas, permitindo a identificação de gargalos e formulação de um plano de ação para melhoria.
limitations: A qualidade incompleta e inconsistente dos dados de log de eventos, oriundos de diferentes fontes com granularidades distintas, representa uma limitação. Outro desafio é o fator humano na implementação do processo, onde atividades podem ser executadas de forma diferente conforme o conhecimento e experiência de quem as realiza.

title: Mining Software Development Process Variations
authors: Renata M. S. Santos; T. Oliveira; Fernando Brito e Abreu
venue: ACM Symposium on Applied Computing
journal_name: Proceedings of the 30th Annual ACM Symposium on Applied Computing
research_question: Como usar process mining para identificar variações na execução de processos de software que são candidatas a tailoring? Quais elementos do processo diferem do fluxo padrão em execuções reais?
main_finding: A técnica proposta utiliza process mining para descobrir e analisar variações em traces de execução que divergem do processo padrão definido, identificando elementos candidatos a tailoring. A abordagem foi validada em um cenário real de desenvolvimento de um grande sistema para institutos federais brasileiros de educação, ciência e tecnologia.
limitations: não_mencionado

title: Software Development Process Mining: Discovery, Conformance Checking and Enhancement
authors: João Caldeira; Fernando Brito e Abreu
venue: Quality of Information and Communications Technology
journal_name: 2016 10th International Conference on the Quality of Information and Communications Technology (QUATIC)
research_question: Como aplicar técnicas de process mining para descoberta, verificação de conformance e melhoria de processos de desenvolvimento de software?
main_finding: A dissertação propõe uma abordagem integrada de process mining para descoberta automática de processos de desenvolvimento de software, verificação de conformidade com processos definidos e sugestões de melhorias baseadas em análise de dados históricos de repositórios de controle de versão.
limitations: Não mencionado nas páginas iniciais fornecidas.

title: Anvaya: An Algorithm and Case-Study on Improving the Goodness of Software Process Models Generated by Mining Event-Log Data in Issue Tracking Systems
authors: Prerna Juneja; D. Kundra; A. Sureka
venue: Annual International Computer Software and Applications Conference
journal_name: 2016 IEEE 40th Annual Computer Software and Applications Conference (COMPSAC)
abstract: Issue Tracking Systems (ITS) such as Bugzilla can be viewed as Process Aware Information Systems (PAIS) generating event-logs during the life-cycle of a bug report. Process Mining consists of mining event logs generated from PAIS for process model discovery, conformance and enhancement. We apply process map discovery techniques to mine event trace data generated from ITS of open source Firefox browser project to generate and study process models. Bug life-cycle consists of diversity and variance. Therefore, the process models generated from the event-logs are spaghetti-like with large number of edges, inter-connections and nodes. Such models are complex to analyse and difficult to comprehend by a process analyst. We improve the Goodness (fitness and structural complexity) of the process models by splitting the event-log into homogeneous subsets by clustering structurally similar traces. We adapt the K-Medoid clustering algorithm with two different distance metrics: Longest Common Subsequence (LCS) and Dynamic Time Warping (DTW). We evaluate the goodness of the process models generated from the clusters using complexity and fitness metrics. We study back-forth and self-loops, bug reopening, and bottleneck in the clusters obtained and show that clustering enables better analysis. We also propose an algorithm to automate the clustering process-the algorithm takes as input the event log and returns the best cluster set.
research_question: Como melhorar a qualidade (fitness e complexidade estrutural) dos modelos de processo de software gerados pela mineração de logs de eventos em sistemas de rastreamento de issues através de clustering de traces?
main_finding: O algoritmo Anvaya baseado em clustering de K-Medoid com métricas de distância LCS e DTW reduz a complexidade estrutural dos modelos de processo spaghetti gerados a partir de logs de rastreamento de issues. O clustering de traces homogêneas melhora a compreensibilidade dos modelos e permite melhor análise de padrões como back-forth, self-loops, reabertura de issues e gargalos.
limitations: O estudo foi conduzido em um único projeto open-source (Firefox) e não especifica comparações quantitativas detalhadas entre as métricas de distância ou validação cruzada com outros datasets.

title: Test Case Generation and Prioritization: A Process-Mining Approach
authors: Andrea Janes
venue: International Conference on Software Testing, Verification and Validation Workshops
journal_name: 2017 IEEE International Conference on Software Testing, Verification and Validation Workshops (ICSTW)
research_question: Como usar process mining para gerar e priorizar casos de teste baseado no comportamento real dos usuários com o software?
main_finding: Process mining pode ser aplicado para minerar modelos de processos a partir de logs de interação do usuário com a interface, permitindo identificar quais funcionalidades são mais utilizadas e com qual frequência para priorizar casos de teste e gerar novos testes baseados em padrões de comportamento real.
limitations: O paper é preliminar e apresenta um estudo inicial; a decisão de reestruturação de elementos de interface depende de mais aspectos além do número de cliques para atingir uma funcionalidade.

title: Monitoring the Software Development Process with Process Mining
authors: Saimir Bala; J. Mendling
venue: International Symposium on Business Modeling and Software Design
research_question: Como o Process Mining pode ser aplicado para monitorar e analisar o processo de desenvolvimento de software em tempo real?
main_finding: O paper apresenta uma abordagem para aplicar Process Mining no monitoramento de processos de desenvolvimento de software, integrando conceitos de modelagem empresarial com engenharia de software para melhor compreensão e otimização dos processos.
limitations: não_mencionado

title: Assessing Agile Software Development Processes with Process Mining: A Case Study
authors: Rita Marques; M. Silva; D. R. Ferreira
venue: Conference on Business Informatics
journal_name: 2018 IEEE 20th Conference on Business Informatics (CBI)
abstract: Software development is a growing industry with increasingly more complex projects. Agile methodologies, such as Scrum, have emerged in part to address this challenge. Agile focuses on a set of best practices rather than a standardized process, which makes it difficult to assess whether it is being properly implemented in an organization. In this work, we analyze the feasibility of using process mining to evaluate the implementation of Scrum practices based on event logs collected from a case-handling system. A case study was conducted in an IT organization that uses Jira Software to manage its projects. With process mining, it was possible to extract the workflow behaviour in two different projects. While Scrum standards like role distribution could be discovered, other practices like customer collaboration could not be detected in the log. This case study provided important insights regarding the application of process mining in case-handling systems and Scrum processes.
research_question: Quais práticas recomendadas do Scrum podem ser descobertas usando técnicas de process mining e quais questões devem ser consideradas ao extrair e analisar event logs com comportamento não estruturado de processos Scrum?
main_finding: O process mining foi capaz de extrair comportamento de workflow em dois projetos diferentes e descobrir padrões Scrum como distribuição de papéis, porém práticas como colaboração com cliente não foram detectadas nos logs. O estudo forneceu insights importantes sobre a aplicação de process mining em sistemas case-handling e processos Scrum.
limitations: Outras práticas do Scrum como colaboração com cliente não puderam ser detectadas nos logs extraídos, limitando o escopo de avaliação completa das práticas ágeis através de process mining.

title: Evaluating Coding Behavior in Software Development Processes: A Process Mining Approach
authors: P. Ardimento; M. Bernardi; Marta Cimitile; F. Maggi
venue: International Conference on Software and Systems Process
journal_name: 2019 IEEE/ACM International Conference on Software and System Processes (ICSSP)
abstract: Process mining is a family of techniques that aim at analyzing business process execution data recorded in event logs. Conformance checking is a branch of this discipline embracing approaches for verifying whether the behavior of a process, as recorded in a log, is in line with some expected behavior provided in the form of a process model. In the literature, process mining techniques have already been used to study software development processes starting from logs derived from version management systems or from document management systems. In this paper, we use conformance checking to test coding behaviors starting from event logs generated from IDE usage. Understanding how developers carry out coding activities and what hurdles they usually face should provide useful tips for improving and supporting software development processes. In particular, through conformance checking, we can compare different process executions, and identify behavioral similarities and differences. In our experimentation, we evaluated the activities performed by 40 novice developers performing coding activities in 5 development sessions. We assessed the developers to distinguish the ones obtaining the best performance. We then compared the behavior extracted from this group of developers with the others. The results show different IDE usage patterns for developers with different skills and performance.
research_question: Como os padrões de comportamento de codificação de desenvolvedores variam entre aqueles com diferentes níveis de desempenho, e como técnicas de process mining podem identificar essas diferenças?
main_finding: O estudo identificou padrões distintos de uso do IDE entre desenvolvedores de alto e baixo desempenho através de análise conformance checking. Os resultados mostram que técnicas de process mining baseadas em Directly Follows Graphs podem revelar diferenças significativas nos comportamentos de codificação que diferenciam desenvolvedores experientes de novatos.
limitations: O estudo envolve apenas 40 estudantes em um contexto acadêmico avançado de Java em 5 sessões de desenvolvimento. A generalização para contextos industriais e diferentes tecnologias não é explorada.

title: Assessing Software Development Teams' Efficiency using Process Mining
authors: João Caldeira; Fernando Brito e Abreu; J. Reis; J. Cardoso
venue: International Conference on Process Mining
journal_name: 2019 International Conference on Process Mining (ICPM)
abstract: Context. Improving the efficiency and effectiveness of software development projects implies understanding their actual process. Given the same requirements specification, different software development teams may follow different strategies and that may lead to inappropriate use of tools or non-optimized allocation of effort on spurious activities, non-aligned with the desired goals. However, due to its intangibility, the actual process followed by each developer or team is often a black box. Objective. The overall goal of this study is to improve the knowledge on how to measure efficiency in development teams where a great deal of variability may exist due to the human-factor. The main focus is on the discovery of the underlying processes and compare them in terms of efficiency and effectiveness. By doing so, we expect to reveal potentially hidden costs and risks, so that corrective actions may take place on a timely manner during the software project life cycle. Method. Several independent teams of Java programmers, using the Eclipse IDE, were assigned the same software quality task, related to code smells detection for identifying refactoring opportunities and the quality of the outcomes were assessed by independent experts. The events corresponding to the activity of each team upon the IDE, while performing the given task, were captured. Then, we used process mining techniques to discover development process models, evaluate their quality and compare variants against a reference model used as "best practice". Results. Teams whose process model was less complex, had the best outcomes and vice-versa. Comparing less complex process variants with the ""best practice"" process, showed that they were also the ones with less differences in the control-flow perspective, based on activities frequencies. We have also determined which teams were most efficient through process analysis. Conclusions. We confirmed that, even for a well-defined software development task, there may be a great deal of process variability due to the human factor. We were able to identify when developers were more or less focused in the essential tasks they were required to perform. Less focused teams had the more complex process models, due to the spurious / non-essential actions that were carried out. In other words, they were less efficient. Experts' opinion confirmed that those teams also were less effective in their expected delivery. We therefore concluded that a self-awareness of the performed process rendered by our approach, may be used to identify corrective actions that will improve process efficiency (less wasted effort) and may yield to better deliverables, i.e. improved process effectiveness.
research_question: Como medir e comparar a eficiência de equipes de desenvolvimento de software através da análise dos processos reais que elas seguem, especialmente considerando a variabilidade introduzida pelo fator humano?
main_finding: Equipes cujos modelos de processo eram menos complexos apresentaram melhores resultados. A comparação com modelos de 'melhor prática' mostrou que processos menos complexos tinham menos diferenças na perspectiva de fluxo de controle. Foi possível identificar quais equipes eram mais eficientes através da análise de processos.
limitations: Estudo realizado com uma tarefa bem definida de detecção de code smells em programadores Java usando Eclipse IDE, o que pode limitar a generalização dos resultados para outros tipos de tarefas ou contextos de desenvolvimento.

title: A User Evaluation of Process Discovery Algorithms in a Software Engineering Company
authors: S. Agostinelli; F. Maggi; Andrea Marrella; Fredrik P. Milani
venue: IEEE International Enterprise Distributed Object Computing Conference
journal_name: 2019 IEEE 23rd International Enterprise Distributed Object Computing Conference (EDOC)
abstract: Process mining methods allow analysts to use logs of historical executions of business processes in order to gain knowledge about the actual behavior of these processes. One of the most widely studied process mining operations is automated process discovery. An event log is taken as input by an automated process discovery method and produces a business process model as output that captures the control-flow relations between tasks that are described by the event log. In this setting, this paper provides a systematic comparative evaluation of existing implementations of automated process discovery methods with domain experts by using a real life event log extracted from an international software engineering company and four quality metrics: understandability, correctness, precision, and usefulness. The evaluation results highlight gaps and unexplored trade-offs in the field and allow researchers to improve the lacks in the automated process discovery methods in terms of usability of process discovery techniques in industry.
research_question: Qual algoritmo de descoberta de processos é percebido como o melhor por especialistas de domínio em uma empresa de engenharia de software?
main_finding: O estudo avalia algoritmos de descoberta de processos através da percepção de especialistas de domínio usando métricas de compreensibilidade, correção, precisão e utilidade. Os resultados destacam lacunas e trade-offs inexplorados na descoberta automatizada de processos em contexto industrial de software.
limitations: Avaliação limitada a uma única empresa de engenharia de software; modelos produzidos frequentemente são grandes e complexos (spaghetti-like), dificultando a adoção prática.

title: DEVELOPMENT OF A TOOLKIT FOR ANALYZING SOFTWARE DEBUGGING PROCESSES USING THE CONSTRUCTIVE APPROACH
authors: V. Shynkarenko; Oleksandr Zhevaho
venue: Eastern-European Journal of Enterprise Technologies
journal_name: Eastern-European Journal of Enterprise Technologies
abstract: Constructive-synthesizing modeling and the Process Mining methods in a toolkit to monitor and analyze the software debugging process were applied. Methods for monitoring the development and debugging processes are the basis for improving the level of practical training of students, reducing the time that is used irrationally in the process of software development by a student, and in monitoring the processes of performance of tasks by a teacher. The process of software debugging is seen as a sequence of actions when dealing with relevant tools. Using the methodology of constructive-synthesizing modeling, a constructor for forming a debugging actions log was developed. Based on the constructive model, the extension to the integrated development environment (IDE) Microsoft Visual Studio, in which all debugging actions are recorded in an event log, was designed. During debugging in the IDE, event logs are collected and then a conformance checking of these logs with regard to the reference model is performed. To do this, the ProM (Eindhoven Technical University, Netherlands), a platform for Process Mining methods, is used. By checking compliance, it is possible to compare different debugging processes and recognize behavioral similarities and differences. The main purpose of the developed toolkit is to collect debugging actions from the developer’s IDE. By better understanding how students grasp and deal with errors, one can help novices learn to program. Knowing how programmers debug can encourage researchers to develop more practically directed methods, enable teachers to improve their debugging curricula and allow tool developers to adapt the debugger to the actual needs of users. It is practically suggested to use the prepared tools in the software engineering course
research_question: Como analisar e entender processos de debugging de software através de Process Mining e modelagem construtiva para melhorar o treinamento de estudantes em programação?
main_finding: Desenvolveu-se um toolkit integrado ao Microsoft Visual Studio que coleta ações de debugging em logs de eventos e realiza conformance checking com modelos de referência usando ProM. A abordagem permite comparar e analisar processos de debugging de diferentes estudantes, identificando similaridades e diferenças comportamentais para melhorar o aprendizado de programação.
limitations: não_mencionado

title: Quantitative evaluation of software component behavior discovery approach
authors: Cong Liu
venue: IEICE Trans. Inf. Syst.
journal_name: IEICE Trans. Inf. Syst.
abstract: SUMMARY During the execution of software systems, their execution data can be recorded. By fully exploiting these data, software practitioners can discover behavioral models describing the actual execution of the underlying software system. The recorded unstructured software execution data may be too complex, spanning over several days, etc. Applying existing discovery techniques results in spaghetti-like models with no clear structure and no valuable information for comprehension. Starting from the observation that a software system is composed of a set of logical components, Liu et al. propose to decompose the software behavior discovery problem into smaller independent ones by discovering a behavioral model per component in [1]. However, the e ﬀ ectiveness of the proposed approach is not fully evaluated and compared with existing approaches. In this paper, we evaluate the quality (in terms of understandability / complexity) of discovered component behavior models in a quantitative manner. Based on evaluation, we show that this approach can reduce the complexity of the discovered model and gives a better understanding.
research_question: Como avaliar quantitativamente a qualidade e efetividade de uma abordagem de descoberta de comportamento de componentes de software em relação à complexidade e compreensibilidade dos modelos descobertos?
main_finding: A abordagem que explora informações de componentes e estrutura hierárquica consegue descobrir modelos comportamentais com melhor compreensibilidade e reduz significativamente a complexidade em comparação com técnicas de descoberta de processo existentes que geram modelos tipo spaghetti.
limitations: Avaliação realizada em apenas um caso de estudo de software acadêmico com dois componentes; efetividade em sistemas de larga escala com múltiplos componentes complexos não foi avaliada.

title: Understanding Coding Behavior: An Incremental Process Mining Approach
authors: P. Ardimento; M. Bernardi; Marta Cimitile; Domenico Redavid; S. Ferilli
venue: Electronics
journal_name: Electronics
abstract: Capturing and analyzing interaction data in real-time from development environments can help in understanding how programmers handle coding activities. We propose the use of process mining to learn coding behavior from event logs captured from a customized Integrated Development Environment, concerning interactions with both such an environment and a Version Control System. In particular, by using an incremental approach, the discovered model can be refined after every single development session, which avoids the need to for the model to learn from scratch from previous sessions. It would also allow one to provide the programmer timely suggestions to improve their performance. In this paper, we applied off-line incremental behavior, so as to be able to analyze it at several levels of depth and at different moments. As a preliminary evaluation of our approach, we investigated the coding activities of six novice students of a Java academic programming course working on a programming case study. The results provide some useful information about the initial difficulties in coding activities faced by programmers and show that their coding behavior could be considered as “formed” after a development task requiring approximately 4000 rows of code.
research_question: Como entender o comportamento de codificação de programadores novatos através da mineração de processos aplicada a logs de eventos capturados de ambientes de desenvolvimento integrados e sistemas de controle de versão?
main_finding: A abordagem de mineração de processos incremental permite refinar modelos de comportamento de codificação após cada sessão de desenvolvimento. O comportamento de codificação de programadores novatos é considerado 'formado' após uma tarefa de desenvolvimento que requer aproximadamente 4000 linhas de código.
limitations: O estudo foi realizado com apenas seis estudantes novatos em um curso de programação Java academicamente controlado, limitando a generalização dos resultados. A avaliação é preliminar e aplicada offline.

title: Software Process Evaluation from User Perceptions and Log Data
authors: Damjan Vavpotič; Saimir Bala; J. Mendling; T. Hovelja
venue: J. Softw. Evol. Process.
journal_name: Journal of Software: Evolution and Process
abstract: Companies often claim to follow specific software development methodologies (SDM) when performing their software development process. These methodologies are often supported by dedicated tools that keep track of work activities carried out by developers. The purpose of this paper is to provide a novel approach that integrates analytical insights from both the perceptions of SDM stakeholders and software development tools logs to provide SDM improvement recommendations. This paper develops a new process improvement approach that combines two significantly different sources of data on the same phenomenon. First, it uses a questionnaire to gather software development stakeholder SDM perceptions (managers and developers). Second, it leverages process mining to analyze software development tools logs to obtain additional information on software development activities. Finally, it develops recommendations based on concurrent analysis of both sources. Our novel process improvement approach is evaluated in three directions: Does the presented approach (RQ1) enable managers to gain additional insights into employees' performance, (RQ2) deliver additional insights into project performance, and (RQ3) enable development of additional SDM improvement recommendations? We find that integrated analysis of software development perception data and software development tools logs opens new possibilities to more precisely identify and improve specific SDM elements. The evaluation of our novel process improvement approach follows a single case study design. Our approach can only be used in enterprises in which software development tools logs are available. The study should be repeated in different cultural settings. We practically show how concurrently analyzing data about developer SDM perceptions and event log data from software development tools enables management to gain additional insights in the software development process regarding the performance of individual developers. The main theoretical contribution of our paper is a novel process improvement approach that effectively integrates data from management and developer perspectives and software development tools logs.
research_question: Em que medida a análise concorrente de percepções de stakeholders sobre metodologias de desenvolvimento de software e dados de logs de ferramentas de desenvolvimento fornece insights adicionais para avaliação e melhoria de processos de software?
main_finding: A abordagem integrada de análise de dados de percepção de desenvolvedores e logs de ferramentas de desenvolvimento permite que a gerência ganhe insights adicionais sobre o desempenho de processos de software e elementos específicos de metodologias de desenvolvimento. A análise concorrente de ambas as fontes de informação permite identificar e desenvolver recomendações de melhoria de SDM que seriam indisponíveis usando apenas uma fonte.
limitations: O estudo foi conduzido como um caso único em uma empresa de SME de desenvolvimento de software austríaca, limitando a generalização. O estudo só pode ser aplicado em empresas onde logs de ferramentas de desenvolvimento estão disponíveis e deve ser repetido em diferentes contextos culturais.

title: Process Mining Model Integrated with Control Flow, Case, Organizational and Time Perspectives in a Software Development Project
authors: Silvia Jaqueline Urrea-Contreras; B. Flores-Rios; F. F. González-Navarro; M. A. Astorga-Vargas; J. Ibarra-Esquer; I. G. Pacheco; C. L. P. Agüero
venue: 2022 10th International Conference in Software Engineering Research and Innovation (CONISOFT)
journal_name: 2022 10th International Conference in Software Engineering Research and Innovation (CONISOFT)
abstract: This paper presents the mining of a software development project from the event logs stored in the software project management systems Issue Tracking System (Jira) and Version Control System (GitHub) to query commits. In addition, a private system owned by the small software development organization was also included that provides for the event streams stored in Jira and GitHub. The mined project implements the approach to integrated model covering the Process Mining perspectives to validate the execution of activities according to the very small software company's process definition, discover deviations and establish corrective actions based on the findings obtained through the control flow, organizational, case, and time perspectives.
research_question: Como integrar múltiplas perspectivas de Process Mining (fluxo de controle, organizacional, caso e tempo) para validar a execução de atividades em um projeto de desenvolvimento de software?
main_finding: O trabalho apresenta um modelo integrado de Process Mining que cobre perspectivas de fluxo de controle, organizacional, caso e tempo para validar a execução de atividades em projetos de desenvolvimento de software. A abordagem extrai eventos de logs armazenados em sistemas de gerenciamento de projetos (Jira e GitHub) para descobrir desvios e estabelecer ações corretivas baseadas nas descobertas.
limitations: O paper não menciona limitações específicas nas páginas fornecidas. A pesquisa aborda especificamente uma pequena empresa de desenvolvimento de software, o que pode limitar a generalização dos resultados.

title: Supporting software evolution actions with process mining
research_question: Como process mining pode apoiar ações de evolução de software conectando desalinhamentos de processos de negócio identificados durante iniciativas de PM com requisitos concretos de software?
main_finding: O paper propõe um schema geral que conecta questões de pesquisa formuladas em iniciativas de PM com ações de evolução de software. A aplicação em um Sistema de Gerenciamento de Documentos Eletrônicos demonstrou como PM pode identificar desalinhamentos de processos e gerar guias de ação concretos para evolução de software, além de identificar desafios específicos da aplicação de PM em EDMS.
limitations: O trabalho apresenta uma aplicação inicial da abordagem em um único domínio (EDMS) de uma instituição acadêmica. A generalização dos resultados para outros contextos e tipos de sistemas de informação não é discutida de forma extensiva.

title: Process Mining from Jira Issues at a Large Company
authors: Bavo Coremans; Arjen L. Klomp; Satrio Adi Rukmono; Jacob Krüger; Dirk Fahland; M. Chaudron
venue: IEEE International Conference on Software Maintenance and Evolution
journal_name: 2023 IEEE International Conference on Software Maintenance and Evolution (ICSME)
abstract: Maintaining a software system is a continuous and complex process, typically following a workflow defined by the responsible organization. However, in practice, developers often deviate from the defined process due to personal preferences, varying customer requirements, or urgent deadlines. Such deviations may cause problems later on, or they may indicate potential process improvements. To deal with deviations and improve processes, it is first necessary to fully understand the processes actually employed by developers. For this purpose, process-mining techniques have been proposed that primarily build on version-control data. In this paper, we present a complementary process-mining technique that uses Jira issues to recover process activities not visible in version-control data, particularly focusing on developers’ interactions with issues and each other. We conducted a case study with 74 repositories of 24 developer teams from one large international company to understand the technique’s merits. Our technique revealed process differences across teams and depending on the types of Jira issues, providing novel insights for the company that helped to better understand the employed processes.
research_question: Como extrair e analisar processos de software a partir de dados de issues do Jira para entender as atividades de desenvolvimento que não são visíveis em sistemas de controle de versão?
main_finding: A técnica proposta permite descobrir atividades de processo e interações entre desenvolvedores a partir de issues do Jira, revelando diferenças nos processos empregados entre 24 equipes de desenvolvimento em 74 repositórios. Os resultados demonstram que o Jira é uma fonte complementar valiosa para process mining, capturando informações de urgência, responsabilidades e status que não estão visíveis no controle de versão.
limitations: O estudo foi conduzido em uma única empresa (Thermo Fisher Scientific), o que pode limitar a generalização dos resultados. O texto não menciona explicitamente outras limitações específicas da técnica.

title: Mining Pull Requests to Detect Process Anomalies in Open Source Software Development
authors: Bohan Liu; He Zhang; Weigang Ma; Hongyu Kuang; Yi Yang; Jinwei Xu; Shan Gao; Jian-Hua Gao
venue: International Conference on Software Engineering
journal_name: 2024 IEEE/ACM 46th International Conference on Software Engineering (ICSE)
abstract: Trustworthy Open Source Software (OSS) development processes are the basis that secures the long-term trustworthiness of soft-ware projects and products. With the aim to investigate the trust-worthiness of the Pull Request (PR) process, the common model of collaborative development in OSS community, we exploit process mining to identify and analyze the normal and anomalous patterns of PR processes, and propose our approach to identifying anomalies from both control-flow and semantic aspects, and then to analyze and synthesize the root causes of the identified anomalies. We analyze 17531 PRs of 18 OSS projects on GitHub, extracting 26 root causes of control-flow anomalies and 19 root causes of semantic anomalies. We find that most PRs can hardly contain both semantic anomalies and control-flow anomalies, and the internal custom rules in projects may be the key causes for the identified anomalous PRs. We further discover and analyze the patterns of normal PR processes. We find that PRs in the non-fork model (42%) are far more likely than the fork model (5%) to bypass the review process, indicating a higher potential risk. Besides, we analyzed nine poisoned projects whose PR practices were indeed worse. Given the complex and diverse PR processes in OSS community, the proposed approach can help identify and understand not only anomalous PRs but also normal PRs, which offers early risk indications of suspicious incidents (such as poisoning) to OSS supply chain.
research_question: Como detectar anomalias em processos de desenvolvimento de software de código aberto através da mineração de Pull Requests? Quais padrões de comportamento anômalo podem ser identificados em fluxos de controle e semântica de processos?
main_finding: O estudo propõe uma abordagem sistemática para minerar Pull Requests e detectar anomalias em processos de desenvolvimento, classificando casos anômalos em quasi-normais, quasi-anormais e anormais através de análise de fluxo de controle e semântica. A metodologia integra conformance checking e descoberta de padrões para identificar desvios em processos de software open source.
limitations: O texto disponível não apresenta seção clara de limitações. A avaliação depende da validação em múltiplos repositórios open source.

title: Applying Process Mining: The Reality of a Software Development SME
authors: Silvia Jaqueline Urrea-Contreras; M. A. Astorga-Vargas; B. Flores-Rios; J. Ibarra-Esquer; F. F. González-Navarro; Ivan Antonio García Pacheco; Carla Leninca Pacheco Agüero
venue: Applied Sciences
journal_name: Applied Sciences
abstract: One of the challenges the organizations confront is to extract data from the information systems to know the reality of their processes to improve their efficiency. In this study, the application of Process Mining is addressed as an opportunity in the specific context of an SME dedicated to software development, implementing the L* life cycle model methodology from a layered Software Engineering approach. This research is carried out based on process improvement in an initial SME project. Subsequently, it is compared with a second project, using different Process Mining perspectives such as control flow, case, organization, and time, with the aim of extending the process model. This holistic view allows not only to better understand the processes involved, but also to identify and analyze the similarities and differences between the two projects. As a result, the Process Mining analysis shows crucial aspects such as the representation of integrated models, traces on sequences of actions, and the interaction of activities with specific roles and deviations in the flow of activities that compromise the quality of the process and the product. At the same time, the challenges that emerged during the improvement cycle are highlighted. These challenges cover issues such as data extraction, fluid communication between those involved, and the documentation associated with the processes. This study contributes to the body of knowledge of Process Mining. Likewise, the case study results offer a vision for other SMEs seeking to incorporate Process Mining as part of their improvement strategies.
research_question: Como aplicar Process Mining em uma PME de desenvolvimento de software para extrair dados de sistemas de informação e comparar processos documentados com processos observados?
main_finding: O estudo demonstra a aplicação prática de Process Mining em uma PME de software usando a metodologia L* lifecycle, identificando aspectos críticos como representação de modelos integrados, sequências de ações e desvios de fluxo. Os principais desafios encontrados incluem extração de dados, comunicação fluida entre envolvidos e documentação inadequada dos processos.
limitations: O trabalho não especifica completamente as ferramentas utilizadas. As limitações reportadas cobrem problemas de extração de dados dispersos em múltiplos sistemas sem relacionamento explícito entre as atividades de processo.

title: Architecting software monitors for control-flow anomaly detection through large language models and conformance checking
authors: Francesco Vitale; Francesco Flammini; M. Caporuscio; N. Mazzocca
venue: Information and Software Technology
journal_name: ArXiv
abstract: Context: Ensuring high levels of dependability in modern computer-based systems has become increasingly challenging due to their complexity. Although systems are validated at design time, their behavior can be different at runtime, possibly showing control-flow anomalies due to ``unknown unknowns''. Objective: We aim to detect control-flow anomalies through software monitoring, which verifies runtime behavior by logging software execution and detecting deviations from expected control flow. Methods: We propose a methodology to develop software monitors for control-flow anomaly detection through Large Language Models (LLMs) and conformance checking. The methodology builds on existing software development practices to maintain traditional V\&V while providing an additional level of robustness and trustworthiness. It leverages LLMs to link design-time models and implementation code, automating source-code instrumentation. The resulting event logs are analyzed via conformance checking, an explainable and effective technique for control-flow anomaly detection. Results: We test the methodology on a case-study scenario from the European Railway Traffic Management System / European Train Control System (ERTMS/ETCS), which is a railway standard for modern interoperable railways. The results obtained from the ERTMS/ETCS case study demonstrate that LLM-based source-code instrumentation can achieve up to 82.849% control-flow coverage of the reference design-time process model, while the subsequent conformance checking-based anomaly detection reaches a peak performance of 95.957% F1-score and 93.669% AUC. Conclusion: Incorporating domain-specific knowledge to guide LLMs in source-code instrumentation significantly allowed obtaining reliable and quality software logs and enabled effective control-flow anomaly detection through conformance checking.
research_question: Como detectar anomalias de fluxo de controle em software através de monitoramento em tempo de execução, combinando Large Language Models para instrumentação de código e conformance checking para análise de logs?
main_finding: A metodologia proposta alcançou 82,849% de cobertura de fluxo de controle através de instrumentação de código baseada em LLM, e a detecção de anomalias via conformance checking atingiu 95,957% F1-score e 93,669% AUC no caso de estudo ERTMS/ETCS. A incorporação de conhecimento específico do domínio para guiar LLMs na instrumentação de código permitiu obter logs de software confiáveis e qualidade.
limitations: As limitações reportadas não foram completamente especificadas no trecho disponível do paper, embora o texto indique desafios práticos em manter consistência entre modelos de sistema e suas implementações.

title: Automating Process Discovery through Event-Data Analysis
authors: J. Cook; A. Wolf
venue: International Conference on Software Engineering
journal_name: 1995 17th International Conference on Software Engineering
research_question: Como automatizar a descoberta de modelos formais de processos de software a partir de dados de eventos coletados durante a execução de processos?
main_finding: O paper apresenta três métodos automatizados para descoberta de processos: inferência de gramática algorítmica, modelos de Markov e redes neurais. Estes métodos permitem gerar modelos de máquinas de estado finito a partir de dados de eventos, reduzindo barreiras de entrada para adoção de tecnologias de modelagem de processos formais.
limitations: Os métodos se concentram apenas em descobrir aspectos comportamentais dos processos, não cobrindo modelos de artefatos, papéis e responsabilidades. Todos os métodos requerem orientação de especialistas em processos de software através de parâmetros de ajuste.

title: Detecting Bottleneck and Fraud in Agile Development by using Petri net Performance and Trace Clustering
authors: Muhammad A. Razi; K. Sungkono; R. Sarno; Cahyaningtyas Sekar Wahyuni
venue: 2019 International Seminar on Application for Technology of Information and Communication (iSemantic)
journal_name: 2019 International Seminar on Application for Technology of Information and Communication (iSemantic)
abstract: Software Development Life Cycle model is one of the basic idea of the software development. SDLC is a continuous process, the cycle starts from the moment when it is decide to release the project, and it ends when its full remove from the exploitation. Agile is one of the mostly used SDLC models. Combination of incremental and iterative process models with a focus on process customer satisfaction and can adapt to rapid delivery of working sofware is called Agile SDLC. This contribution of this paper is evaluating the performance, such as occurring bottleneck and fraud, of an Agile model to handle the management of the project. This paper uses Petri net to analyze Agile SDLC model performance. The petri net use to detect the bottleneck that occur in the model. If an event has longer waiting time tahen others, it can identify that the bottleneck occur in that task. Then, the fraud is detected based on trace clustering. Based on the experiment, the cluster which has lowest member is indicates as fraud processes.
research_question: Como detectar gargalos e fraudes em desenvolvimento Agile usando análise de desempenho de Redes de Petri e clustering de traces?
main_finding: O paper propõe uma abordagem usando Redes de Petri para detectar gargalos através da análise de tempos de espera em tarefas do processo Agile, e identifica fraudes através de clustering de traces, onde clusters com menor número de membros indicam processos fraudulentos.
limitations: não_mencionado

title: Studying Task Processes for Improving Programmer Productivity
authors: P. Jalote; Damodaram Kamma
venue: IEEE Transactions on Software Engineering
journal_name: IEEE Transactions on Software Engineering
abstract: Productivity of a software development organization can be enhanced by improving the software process, using better tools/technology, and enhancing the productivity of programmers. This work focuses on improving programmer productivity by studying the process used by a programmer for executing an assigned task, which we call the task process. We propose a general framework for studying the impact of task processes on programmer productivity and also the impact of transferring task processes of high-productivity programmers to average-productivity peers. We applied the framework to a few live projects in Robert Bosch Engineering and Business Solutions Limited, a CMMI Level 5 company. In each project, we identified two groups of programmers: high-productivity and average-productivity programmers. We requested each programmer to video capture their computer screen while executing his/her assigned tasks. We then analyzed these task videos to extract the task processes and then used them to identify the differences between the task processes used by the two groups. Some key differences were found between the task processes, which could account for the difference in productivities of the two groups. Similarities between the task processes were also analyzed quantitatively by modeling each task process as a Markov chain. We found that programmers from the same group used similar task processes, but the task processes of the two groups differed considerably. The task processes of high-productivity programmers were transferred to the average-productivity programmers by training them on the key steps missing in their process but commonly present in the work of their high-productivity peers. A substantial productivity gain was found in the average-productivity programmers as a result of this transfer. The study shows that task processes of programmers impact their productivity, and it is possible to improve the productivity of average-productivity programmers by transferring task processes from high-productivity programmers to them.
research_question: Como os processos de tarefas utilizados por programadores afetam sua produtividade? É possível melhorar a produtividade transferindo processos de tarefas de programadores de alta produtividade para seus pares?
main_finding: Programadores de alta produtividade utilizam processos de tarefas diferentes e mais eficientes comparados aos de produtividade média. A transferência dos processos de tarefas dos programadores de alta produtividade para os de produtividade média, através de treinamento, resultou em ganhos substanciais de produtividade. Os processos de tarefas foram modelados como cadeias de Markov para análise quantitativa das similaridades.
limitations: Estudo realizado em uma organização específica (CMMI Level 5) e em projetos de modelo baseado em testes unitários. Tamanho da amostra e generalizabilidade dos resultados não mencionados explicitamente no trecho fornecido.

title: Application of Constructive Modeling and Process Mining Approaches to the Study of Source Code Development in Software Engineering Courses
authors: V. Shynkarenko; Oleksandr Zhevaho
venue: Journal of Communications Software and Systems
journal_name: Journal of Communications Software and Systems
abstract: —We present an approach of constructing a source code history for a modern code review. Practically, it is supposed to be used in programming training, especially within initial stages. The developed constructor uses constructive-synthesizing modeling tools to classify a source code history by fine-grained changes and to construct an event log file aimed to provide information on students’ coding process. Current research applies Process Mining techniques to the software engineering domain to identify software engineering skills. By better understanding of the way students design programs, we will help novices learn programming. This research provides an innovative method of using code and development process review in teaching programming skills and is aimed to encourage using code review and monitoring coding practice in educational purposes. The standard method of evaluation takes into consideration only a final result, which doesn’t meet modern requirements of teaching programming.
research_question: Como aplicar Process Mining e modelagem construtiva para analisar o processo de desenvolvimento de código-fonte e identificar habilidades de engenharia de software em estudantes de programação?
main_finding: O estudo apresenta uma abordagem inovadora para análise do processo de desenvolvimento de código-fonte em ambientes educacionais, utilizando modelagem construtiva-sintetizadora para classificar o histórico de código e Process Mining para identificar padrões de comportamento dos programadores, visando melhorar o ensino de habilidades de programação.
limitations: não_mencionado

title: Stochastic conformance checking based on variable-length Markov chains
authors: Emilio Incerto; Andrea Vandin; S. S. Ahrabi
venue: Information Systems
journal_name: Inf. Syst.
abstract: Conformance checking is central in process mining (PM). It studies deviations of logs from reference processes. Originally, the proposed approaches did not focus on stochastic aspects of the underlying process, and gave qualitative models as output. Recently, these have been extended in approaches for stochastic conformance checking (SCC), giving quantitative models as output. A different community, namely the software performance engineering (PE) one, interested in the synthesis of stochastic processes since decades, has developed independently techniques to synthesize Markov Chains (MC) that describe the stochastic process underlying program runs. However, these were never applied to SCC problems. We propose a novel approach to SCC based on PE results for the synthesis of stochastic processes. Thanks to a rich experimental evaluation, we show that it outperforms the state-of-the-art. In doing so, we further bridge PE and PM, fostering cross-fertilization. We use techniques for the synthesis of Variable-length MC (VLMC), higher-order MC able to compactly encode complex path dependencies in the control-flow. VLMCs are equipped with a notion of likelihood that a trace belongs to a model. We use it to perform SCC of a log against a model. We establish the degree of conformance by equipping VLMCs with uEMSC, a standard conformance measure in the SCC literature. We compare with 18 SCC techniques from the PM literature, using 11 benchmark datasets from the PM community. We outperform all approaches in 10 out of 11 datasets, i.e., we get uEMSC values closer to 1 for logs conforming to a model. Furthermore, we show that VLMC are efficient, as they handled all considered datasets in a few seconds.
research_question: Como aplicar técnicas de síntese de cadeias de Markov de comprimento variável (VLMC) do engenharia de desempenho de software para melhorar a verificação de conformidade estocástica em process mining?
main_finding: A abordagem proposta baseada em VLMC supera 18 técnicas de verificação de conformidade estocástica existentes em 10 de 11 datasets de benchmark, alcançando valores de uEMSC mais próximos a 1 para logs conformes. As VLMCs demonstram eficiência, processando todos os datasets em poucos segundos.
limitations: O estudo não é específico para processos de software, focando em aplicações gerais de process mining. A comparação é principalmente com técnicas do domínio de process mining, não explorando outras abordagens de engenharia de desempenho.

title: PRIMAD: Building Digital Twins for Software Development Lifecycle within an Evolutionary Architecture
authors: Miguel A. Guinea-Cabrera; J. A. Holgado-Terriza; Pablo A. Pico-Valencia
venue: 2025 IEEE Smart World Congress (SWC)
journal_name: 2025 IEEE Smart World Congress (SWC)
abstract: Current Digital Twin (DT) adoption in the Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC) faces significant hurdles, including a lack of standardized methods, limited research, and isolated use cases. Optimizing value streams is crucial for modern software organizations, which are increasingly characterized by diverse and evolving roles. Implementing a digital twin in this context can significantly enhance the ability to identify opportunities for improvement and explore potential change scenarios. To overcome these adoption barriers, we propose PRIMAD, a progressive methodology for building an SDLC DT that delivers incremental benefits by leveraging existing empirical data from issue-tracking systems, such as JIRA. This approach resolves the "cold start" problem, enabling simulations to emerge directly from real-world behavior. Our methodology integrates a Python-based simulation core with an Akka actor system for modeling issues and resources, enriched by supervised machine learning for context-aware predictions. Preliminary results validating PRIMAD components independently (using pm4py, Akka, and LightGBM) show promising outcomes. This paper details our methodology, presents preliminary component-wise results, and outlines future work.
research_question: Como construir Digital Twins para o Software Development Lifecycle que superem o problema de 'cold start' e forneçam valor incremental através de dados empíricos de sistemas de rastreamento de issues?
main_finding: PRIMAD é uma metodologia progressiva que constrói Digital Twins para SDLC aproveitando dados empíricos de sistemas de rastreamento como JIRA, integrando simulação baseada em Python com sistemas de atores Akka e aprendizado de máquina supervisionado para previsões contextualmente sensíveis, resultados preliminares mostram aplicabilidade promissora para reduzir ciclo de tempo e acelerar fluxo de valor.
limitations: Resultados são preliminares e validados apenas componente-a-componente; falta validação end-to-end em ambiente de produção e avaliação empírica completa do impacto organizacional.

title: Kanban and Process Mining in the Task Management
authors: Vitor Mendes; Elio Ribeiro Faria Junior; C. Garcia; A. Malucelli
venue: Brazilian Symposium on Software Quality
journal_name: Proceedings of the XVII Brazilian Symposium on Software Quality
abstract: In a world continuously changing with markets that are in permanent evolution and in contend, the companys must inovate, be more flexible, offer quality and respond swiftly. These factors do not apply for product manufacture processes only, but for software development processes aswell. This paper describes an account of experiences with the use of Kanban to improve the activities management in an ERP's development. To do this, a mapping of the current process was carried out, so to propose a set of improvements in the management of activities. As a result, a set of tools were implemented to enable a clear view and control over the activities, as well as to integrate the Issue Tracking System with the Kanban board. Furthermore, a process mining technique was used to estimate the time of conclusion of the activities and to detect performance problem.
research_question: Como aplicar Kanban e mineração de processos para melhorar o gerenciamento de atividades em adaptações de um sistema ERP?
main_finding: A integração de Kanban com mineração de processos permitiu melhorar a gestão de atividades em desenvolvimento de ERP, proporcionando visibilidade transparente das atividades, redução do lead time e identificação de problemas de desempenho através da análise de padrões de execução.
limitations: não_mencionado

title: SIMPT: Process Improvement Using Interactive Simulation of Time-Aware Process Trees
authors: M. Pourbafrani; Shuai Jiao; Wil M.P. van der Aalst
venue: Research Challenges in Information Science
journal_name: ArXiv
abstract: Process mining techniques including process discovery, conformance checking, and process enhancement provide extensive knowledge about processes. Discovering running processes and deviations as well as detecting performance problems and bottlenecks are well-supported by process mining tools. However, all the provided techniques represent the past/current state of the process. The improvement in a process requires insights into the future states of the process w.r.t. the possible actions/changes. In this paper, we present a new tool that enables process owners to extract all the process aspects from their historical event data automatically, change these aspects, and re-run the process automatically using an interface. The combination of process mining and simulation techniques provides new evidence-driven ways to explore"what-if"questions. Therefore, assessing the effects of changes in process improvement is also possible. Our Python-based web-application provides a complete interactive platform to improve the flow of activities, i.e., process tree, along with possible changes in all the derived activity, resource, and process parameters. These parameters are derived directly from an event log without user-background knowledge.
research_question: Como combinar técnicas de process mining e simulação para permitir que proprietários de processos explorem cenários 'what-if' e avaliem o impacto de mudanças em processos de software de forma interativa?
main_finding: O SIMPT é uma ferramenta web-based que integra process mining e simulação para gerar automaticamente modelos de processo executáveis a partir de event logs, permitindo proprietários de processos explorar cenários 'what-if' e avaliar mudanças em parâmetros de atividades, recursos e processos sem conhecimento técnico prévio.
limitations: O artigo apresentado consiste apenas nas primeiras páginas, limitando a identificação completa de limitações reportadas e resultados experimentais detalhados.

title: Unveiling process insights from refactoring practices
authors: João Caldeira; Fernando Brito e Abreu; J. Cardoso; J. Reis
venue: Comput. Stand. Interfaces
journal_name: ArXiv
abstract: Context : Software comprehension and maintenance activities, such as refactoring, are said to be negatively impacted by software complexity. The methods used to measure software product and processes complexity have been thoroughly debated in the literature. However, the discernment about the possible links between these two dimensions, particularly on the benefits of using the process perspective, has a long journey ahead. Objective: To improve the understanding of the liaison of developers' activities and software complexity within a refactoring task, namely by evaluating if process metrics gathered from the IDE, using process mining methods and tools, are suitable to accurately classify different refactoring practices and the resulting software complexity. Method: We mined source code metrics from a software product after a quality improvement task was given in parallel to (117) software developers, organized in (71) teams. Simultaneously, we collected events from their IDE work sessions (320) and used process mining to model their processes and extract the correspondent metrics. Results: Most teams using a plugin for refactoring (JDeodorant) reduced software complexity more effectively and with simpler processes than the ones that performed refactoring using only Eclipse native features. We were able to find moderate correlations (43%) between software cyclomatic complexity and process cyclomatic complexity. The best models found for the refactoring method and cyclomatic complexity level predictions, had an accuracy of 92.95% and 94.36%, respectively. Conclusions: Our approach agnostic to programming languages, geographic location, or development practices. Initial findings are encouraging, and lead us to suggest practitioners may use our method in other development tasks, such as, defect analysis and unit or integration tests.
research_question: Como as métricas de processo extraídas do IDE usando process mining podem classificar diferentes práticas de refatoração e sua relação com a complexidade do software?
tool_used: JDeodorant | Eclipse | process mining tools não especificados
main_finding: Equipes usando plugin de refatoração automática (JDeodorant) reduziram complexidade de software mais efetivamente com processos mais simples. Modelos preditivos baseados em métricas de processo alcançaram 92.95% de acurácia para detectar tipo de refatoração e 94.36% para prever redução de complexidade ciclomática.
limitations: não_mencionado

title: An Intelligent Methodology to Enhance Requirements Engineering in Multidisciplinary Projects
authors: A. Salmani; Aaron Imani; Majid Bahrehvar; L. Duffett-Leger; Mohammad Moshirpour
venue: Canadian Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering
journal_name: 2022 IEEE Canadian Conference on Electrical and Computer Engineering (CCECE)
abstract: The multidisciplinary nature of a team has been identified as one of the success criteria of both user-centric approaches and agile methods. Stakeholder involvement is considered to be essential for agile processes in order to meet project objectives and ensure results are aligned with stakeholder expectations. However, establishing a collaborative process involving designers, programmers, stakeholders, and users can be challenging; particularly during the requirements engineering stage. Agile methodologies, such as scrum, offer a powerful way of effectively managing software projects and generates a great deal of useful data through tools such as Jira and GitHub. The aim of this research is two folds: 1) analyze the project data from aforementioned sources using process mining techniques to discover deficiencies in the software development process, and 2) propose an automated effort estimation process to address the identified challenges in this study and provide decision support in the development process. This approach is applied to a case study of a virtual healthcare intervention system. The results are indicative that these enhancements helped boost the decision-making and release planning processes by providing the development team a more clear picture, which ultimately mitigated the number of change requests.
research_question: Como melhorar a engenharia de requisitos em projetos multidisciplinares usando process mining e estimativa automatizada de esforço?
main_finding: Desafios em projetos multidisciplinares foram causados por comunicação deficiente e falta de conhecimento de domínio. A aplicação de process mining em dados do GitHub e Jira identificou deficiências no processo de desenvolvimento, e uma ferramenta de suporte à decisão automatizada foi desenvolvida para melhorar a estimativa de esforço e reduzir mudanças de requisitos.
limitations: não_mencionado

title: Enhanced What-If Scenarios Generation by Bridging Generative Models and Process Simulation
authors: Andrei Buliga; Francesca Meneghello; Riccardo Graziosi; Massimiliano Ronzani
venue: International Conference on Process Mining
journal_name: 2025 7th International Conference on Process Mining (ICPM)
abstract: Generative Artificial Intelligence (GenAI) techniques have recently emerged as promising tools for challenges in Process Mining such as missing data, small datasets, and the generation of complex what-if scenarios. A major limitation of current approaches is their inability to produce sets of process traces in a mutually dependent manner. Traces are generated independently, failing to reflect crucial process-level phenomena such as concurrency, resource contention, and workload distribution. This limitation significantly reduces the realism and utility of generated logs, yet has received limited attention in the literature. In contrast, Business Process Simulation (BPS) excels at preserving process constraints and interdependencies. However, in the presence of specific or complex what-if scenarios, this requires the adjustment of several simulation parameters, which can be challenging to achieve. Motivated by these issues, where generative methods lack consistency and BPS methods lack flexibility, we propose a novel data generation framework combining GenAI with BPS. The proposed approach enables generating diverse and complex whatif scenarios via data-driven generative models, which are then validated and refined through BPS to ensure consistency and feasibility at the log level. We call the approach What-If Scenarios via Data-driven generative mOdels and siMulation (WISDOM). We compare several existing methods for process data generation and assess their outputs using an established evaluation framework. Results show that while generative methods fail to capture the full intricacies of process behaviour, their integration with BPS produces traces that are both diverse and compliant with process constraints. This hybrid approach addresses a critical gap in the literature and opens new avenues for robust, simulation-grounded generation methods in process mining.
research_question: Como combinar modelos generativos de IA com simulação de processos de negócio para gerar cenários what-if diversos e complexos enquanto se garante a consistência com restrições de processos?
main_finding: A abordagem WISDOM integra modelos generativos de IA com BPS para produzir traces que são simultaneamente diversos e conformes às restrições de processos. Enquanto modelos GenAI falham em capturar dependências inter-trace (como contenção de recursos), a integração com simulação garante realismo e viabilidade das logs geradas para cenários what-if complexos.
limitations: Métodos GenAI atuais não conseguem generalizar além dos comportamentos observados nos dados de treinamento e geram traces independentes sem refletir dependências inter-trace presentes em processos reais. BPS requer ajuste manual de múltiplos parâmetros para cenários what-if complexos.

title: A Systematic Approach to Process Enactment Analysis as Input to Software Process Improvement or Tailoring
authors: Mingkui Huo; He Zhang; R. Jeffery
venue: Asia-Pacific Software Engineering Conference
journal_name: 2006 13th Asia Pacific Software Engineering Conference (APSEC'06)
research_question: Como identificar a lacuna entre o modelo de processo planejado e sua execução real em projetos de software? Como usar process mining para recuperar padrões de processo que suportam melhoria e tailoring?
main_finding: O trabalho apresenta um método de recuperação de modelo de processo que coleta dados de execução de projetos e os analisa para descobrir padrões de processo no mesmo nível de abstração do modelo predefinido. Isso facilita a identificação de discrepâncias entre o processo planejado e a execução real, permitindo melhorias empíricas através de tailoring.
limitations: Dificuldade em coletar dados suficientes de múltiplos projetos que seguem consistentemente um modelo de processo predefinido. Lacuna entre modelos de processo de baixo nível (atividades diárias) e modelos predefinidos de alto nível (como waterfall).

title: Software Process Recovery: Recovering Process from Artifacts
authors: Abram Hindle
venue: Working Conference on Reverse Engineering
journal_name: 2010 17th Working Conference on Reverse Engineering
research_question: É possível recuperar os processos de desenvolvimento de software subjacentes de um projeto analisando automaticamente os artefatos deixados em repositórios como sistemas de controle de versão, rastreadores de bugs e arquivos de listas de email?
main_finding: O trabalho propõe Software Process Recovery, uma abordagem que combina Mining Software Repositories com process mining e process discovery para recuperar processos de desenvolvimento através da análise de artefatos históricos. Utiliza técnicas de machine learning, análise de tópicos e processamento de linguagem natural para gerar Recovered Unified Process Views (RUPV) que mapeiam eventos de processo ao longo do tempo.
limitations: não_mencionado

title: Foundations for Event-Based Process Analysis in Heterogeneous Software Engineering Environments
authors: W. Sunindyo; T. Moser; D. Winkler; S. Biffl
venue: EUROMICRO Conference on Software Engineering and Advanced Applications
journal_name: 2010 36th EUROMICRO Conference on Software Engineering and Advanced Applications
research_question: Como monitorar, controlar e analisar processos de engenharia de software em ambientes heterogêneos usando análise baseada em eventos?
main_finding: A abordagem permitiu comparar processos de engenharia esperados e reais considerando estrutura, performance de passos individuais e risco de gargalos. O sistema foi avaliado usando o processo de 'continuous integration and test' capturando desvios de execução através de logs de eventos.
limitations: O paper não menciona explicitamente limitações técnicas ou de escala, porém indica que o framework necessita extensões para suportar eficientemente o monitoramento e análise baseada em eventos de logs heterogêneos.

title: Software process discovery using link analysis
authors: Beichen Duan; Beijun Shen
venue: IEEE International Conference on Communication Software and Networks
journal_name: 2011 IEEE 3rd International Conference on Communication Software and Networks
research_question: Como descobrir e modelar o fluxo de controle do processo de desenvolvimento de software através da análise de repositórios de código aberto, especificamente utilizando análise de ligações entre artefatos e atividades?
main_finding: O artigo apresenta um framework semi-automático para descobrir o fluxo de controle de processos de software a partir de repositórios, mapeando artefatos (arquivos) para atividades de processo através de análise de ligações. A abordagem processa logs de versionamento em três estágios: obtenção do repositório, geração de arquivo de log de eventos em formato comum, e mapeamento de links entre arquivos e atividades de processo.
