limitations: O artigo menciona potenciais ameaças à validade (seção 5) mas estas não são detalhadas nas páginas fornecidas. A abordagem depende da qualidade e consistência do mapeamento manual de artefatos para atividades.

title: Process Mining Software Repositories
authors: Wouter Poncin; Alexander Serebrenik; M. Brand
venue: European Conference on Software Maintenance and Reengineering
journal_name: 2011 15th European Conference on Software Maintenance and Reengineering
research_question: Como aplicar técnicas de process mining em repositórios de software para analisar processos de desenvolvimento, considerando a necessidade de combinar múltiplos repositórios e correlacionar eventos relacionados?
main_finding: O framework FRASR foi desenvolvido para integrar múltiplos repositórios de software e aplicar process mining, permitindo análise de processos de desenvolvimento como papéis de desenvolvedores e tratamento de relatórios de bugs. A abordagem combina e correlaciona eventos de diferentes fontes para conformidade com o metamodelo de event log do process mining.
limitations: não_mencionado

title: Process Cube for Software Defect Resolution
authors: Monika Gupta; A. Sureka
venue: Asia-Pacific Software Engineering Conference
journal_name: 2014 21st Asia-Pacific Software Engineering Conference
research_question: Como aplicar Process Cube para analisar e comparar o processo de resolução de defeitos em software sob múltiplas dimensões? Como operações OLAP podem facilitar a descoberta de variantes de processo e identificar ineficiências?
main_finding: O trabalho apresenta um framework de Process Cube para análise multidimensional do processo de resolução de defeitos em software, integrando dados de Issue Tracking System, Peer Code Review System e Version Control System. A aplicação de operações OLAP (slice, dice, roll-up, drill-down) permite comparar variantes de processo e identificar gargalos e ineficiências em diferentes perspectivas (fluxo de controle, tempo, conformidade e organizacional).
limitations: não_mencionado

title: Toward using business process intelligence to support incident management metrics selection and service improvement
authors: Bianca Trinkenreich; Gleison Santos; Valdemar T. F. Confort; F. Santoro
venue: International Conference on Software Engineering and Knowledge Engineering
research_question: Qual sub-processo de Incident Management causa mais impacto no tempo de resolução e quais métricas adequadas podem ser utilizadas para medi-lo e melhorá-lo?
main_finding: Ao aplicar BPM, BPI e Process Mining, os autores descobriram o processo subjacente e identificaram um gargalo impactando o tempo de resolução de incidentes. Propuseram métricas para melhorar o processo e qualidade do serviço aplicando GQM+Strategies, convergindo BPM/Process Mining com modelos de maturidade CMMI-SVC.
limitations: Não mencionadas explicitamente nas primeiras páginas fornecidas; estudo focado em uma única organização global de grande porte.

title: Mining project-oriented business processes
authors: Saimir Bala; C. Cabanillas; J. Mendling; Andreas Rogge-Solti; A. Polleres
venue: International Conference on Business Process Management
research_question: Como extrair e visualizar modelos de processos orientados a projetos a partir de dados de sistemas de controle de versão, especialmente quando não há um mecanismo central de controle de processo?
main_finding: O artigo propõe uma técnica de mineração de projetos (project mining) que gera modelos de processos orientados a projetos visualizados como gráficos de Gantt a partir de logs de sistemas de controle de versão. A abordagem formaliza conceitos de processos orientados a projetos e demonstra benefícios em comparação com técnicas de process mining existentes para esta classe específica de processos.
limitations: Limitações não explicitamente mencionadas no texto fornecido, mas a abordagem é aplicada especificamente a projetos de engenharia complexa de longo prazo com múltiplos atores.

title: Process mining for project management
authors: J. Joe; Yasha Ballal; Tanya Emmatty; S. Kulkarni
venue: 2016 International Conference on Data Mining and Advanced Computing (SAPIENCE)
journal_name: 2016 International Conference on Data Mining and Advanced Computing (SAPIENCE)
research_question: Como aplicar process mining para otimizar o gerenciamento de projetos em organizações de TI, extraindo padrões de logs de eventos para melhorar a inteligência de processos de software?
main_finding: O trabalho propõe uma plataforma amigável capaz de gerar modelos baseados em redes de Petri através de process mining, utilizando o algoritmo Heuristic Miner para minerar logs de eventos e otimizar procedimentos de gerenciamento de projetos em organizações de TI, fornecendo análise de workflow e estratégias de trabalho superiores.
limitations: não_mencionado

title: Extracting and Conserving Production Data as Test Cases in Executable Business Process Architectures
authors: Daniel Lübke
journal_name: Procedia Computer Science
abstract: Abstract Because executable business processes are an important and critical software asset of organizations because they control and integrate critical information systems. Thus, testing them thoroughly is a very important task within the software development process. However, failures due to implementation defects still occur in production, which in turn means that the development team needs to analyze, fix and repair the failing processes. In order to support the activities of reproducing the problem outside of the production system and to create better test cases for verifying the fixed implementation, we propose to use process mining techniques on the production process event logs to aid the support & development teams. With our approach it is possible to automatically extract a working unit test case with all partner services being mocked that can run in a development environment. Within in this paper we present the extraction algorithm, our implementation, and possible ways to integrate the tool into the support & development process.
research_question: Como extrair dados de produção de processos de negócio executáveis para criar casos de teste que permitam reproduzir e corrigir falhas sem acessar o ambiente de produção?
main_finding: O trabalho propõe usar técnicas de process mining para extrair dados relevantes de instâncias de processos BPEL em produção e armazená-los em casos de teste de replicação (Replication Test Cases) que permitem ao time de desenvolvimento reproduzir e corrigir falhas em ambiente isolado com serviços mockados.
limitations: não_mencionado

title: Using constraint mining to analyze software development processes
authors: Thomas Krismayer; Christoph Mayr-Dorn; Johann Tuder; R. Rabiser; P. Grünbacher
venue: International Conference on Software and Systems Process
journal_name: 2019 IEEE/ACM International Conference on Software and System Processes (ICSSP)
abstract: Most software development organizations nowadays use issue-tracking tools to manage software processes throughout the life-cycle. Still, understanding development processes, keeping track of process execution, and reacting to deviations in projects remains challenging. In particular, the actual process usually differs from the process perceived by developers, making it hard to define the processes developers are expected to carry out. This is further challenged by frequently changing processes and process variations in different projects and teams. In this paper we describe an empirical study in which we applied a constraint mining approach from the field of software monitoring to automatically extract process definitions in the form of constraints. Specifically, we applied the approach to datasets extracted from four real-world projects (using the Jira issue-tracking tool) in a company developing a recreational activities platform. The mined constraints describe the boundaries of the actual processes and thus help to understand process behavior. Constraints can be frequently re-mined to understand process evolution. The mined constraints can also be used to monitor future processes to detect problems in the development process early on. We involved a domain expert to evaluate the usefulness of our results and investigated to what extent the mined constraints reflect the official development process of the company. We also report mining results for different issue types, across projects, and over different time windows.
research_question: Como técnicas de constraint mining podem ser aplicadas para extrair automaticamente definições de processos a partir de dados de issue-tracking e monitorar desvios em processos de desenvolvimento de software?
main_finding: O constraint mining conseguiu extrair automaticamente definições de processos como restrições sobre sequências de estados, durações e papéis envolvidos em tarefas de desenvolvimento. As restrições mineradas refletem o processo real executado e podem ser utilizadas para monitorar desvios e entender evolução de processos através de re-mineração periódica.
limitations: Estudo limitado a quatro projetos de uma única organização. A avaliação da utilidade dos resultados dependeu da disponibilidade de um especialista de domínio específico.

title: Automatic Real-Time Mining Software Process Activities From SVN Logs Using a Naive Bayes Classifier
authors: Rui Zhu; Yichao Dai; Tong Li; Zifei Ma; Ming Zheng; Yahui Tang; Jiayi Yuan; Yue Huang
venue: IEEE Access
journal_name: IEEE Access
abstract: The abundance of event data in current software configuration management systems makes it possible to discover software process models automatically by using actual observed behavior. However, traditional process mining algorithms cannot be applied to event logs recorded in software configuration management (SCM) systems, such as SVN, because of missing activity attributes. To address this problem, a software process activity classifier is proposed to build event-activity mapping relationships from software development event streams, revealing activity attributes and associating the activity to the original SVN log. The proposed approach extracts activity from the SVN log based on semantic features and introduces a novel technique based on a naive Bayes approach to associate event activities dynamically. The approach has been applied to two real-world software development process logs, ArgoUML and jEdit, consisting of more than 80,000 events, covering development information from 1998 to 2015. With the application of our approach to such data, activities can be extracted from event logs and a classifier can be constructed for adding activity attributes to new events. The results of the classification are evaluated in terms of precision rate, recall rate, and the F-measure. Overall, two real-world software development process logs are used to validate the method, and the experimental results show that the approach can mine software process activities from SVN log events automatically and in real-time.
research_question: Como extrair automaticamente atividades de processos de software a partir de logs SVN em tempo real, considerando a falta de atributos de atividade nesses dados?
tool_used: Word2Vec | K-means clustering | Naive Bayes classifier
main_finding: A abordagem proposta combina aprendizado supervisionado com não supervisionado para extrair automaticamente atividades de logs SVN, utilizando Naive Bayes para classificar eventos. Testado em dois projetos de código aberto (ArgoUML e jEdit) com mais de 80.000 eventos, atingindo altos índices de precisão, recall e F-measure.
limitations: O paper apresentado contém apenas as primeiras páginas, limitando a identificação completa das limitações reportadas pelos autores.

title: Understanding What Software Engineers Are Working on: The Work-Item Prediction Challenge
authors: R. Lämmel; A. Kerber; Liane Praza
venue: IEEE International Conference on Program Comprehension
journal_name: 2020 IEEE/ACM 28th International Conference on Program Comprehension (ICPC)
abstract: Understanding what a software engineer (a developer, an incident responder, a production engineer, etc.) is working on is a challenging problem - especially when considering the more complex software engineering workflows in software-intensive organizations: i) engineers rely on a multitude (perhaps hundreds) of loosely integrated tools; ii) engineers engage in concurrent and relatively long running workflows; ii) infrastructure (such as logging) is not fully aware of work items; iv) engineering processes (e.g., for incident response) are not explicitly modeled. In this paper, we explain the corresponding’ work-item prediction challenge’ on the grounds of representative scenarios, report on related efforts at Facebook, discuss some lessons learned, and review related work to call to arms to leverage, advance, and combine techniques from program comprehension, mining software repositories, process mining, and machine learning.
research_question: Como prever qual item de trabalho um engenheiro de software está realizando em um determinado momento, considerando a natureza distribuída e fracamente integrada dos fluxos de trabalho de engenharia?
main_finding: O artigo caracteriza o desafio de predição de itens de trabalho (WIP challenge) em organizações de engenharia de software intensiva, identificando quatro características críticas: dependência de múltiplas ferramentas fracamente integradas, fluxos de trabalho concorrentes e de longa duração, infraestrutura de logging incompletamente ciente de itens de trabalho, e processos de engenharia não explicitamente modelados. Propõe uma agenda de pesquisa integrando técnicas de compreensão de programas, mineração de repositórios de software, process mining e aprendizado de máquina.
limitations: Não é possível reportar detalhes específicos de cenários reais do Facebook por questões de confidencialidade. O artigo apresenta principalmente uma chamada à ação e discussão de desafios, sem apresentar soluções implementadas ou validadas empiricamente.

title: Mining Software Repositories for the Characterization of Continuous Integration and Delivery
authors: Gabriele Destro; B. D. França
venue: Brazilian Symposium on Software Engineering
journal_name: Proceedings of the XXXIV Brazilian Symposium on Software Engineering
abstract: Continuous Integration (CI) and Delivery (CD) are software development practices increasingly adopted in industry, which aiming at ensuring quality and stability, and making the whole process more efficient and less error-prone. Software engineers that incorporate these practices may have difficulties trying to get an overview of the impact of adopting them and tracking progress. Therefore, performing analysis regarding the level of the application of CI/CD practices in a software repository can help to understand how such practices are adopted, which can result in a better characterization of their benefits and limitations. To support this analysis, we developed Garimpeiro, a web application that supports the characterization of open source repositories hosted on GitHub regarding the level of adoption of CI/CD practices. This paper describes how the tool extracts data from GitHub repositories by showing the execution workflow, high-level architecture, and involved assumptions to define metrics. Finally, this paper also includes running examples of the tool, which demonstrates its capabilities and feasibility.
research_question: Como caracterizar o nível de adoção de práticas de Continuous Integration e Continuous Delivery em repositórios de software de código aberto através da mineração de repositórios?
main_finding: Garimpeiro é uma ferramenta web que suporta a caracterização de repositórios de código aberto hospedados no GitHub quanto ao nível de adoção de práticas CI/CD, extraindo dados sobre tecnologias, procedimentos e artefatos relacionados à integração contínua e entrega. A ferramenta automatiza a coleta de dados de repositórios para análise de CI/CD e atualmente funciona melhor no ecossistema Java, podendo ser estendida para outras plataformas.
limitations: A ferramenta atualmente funciona melhor para o ecossistema Java. Informações sobre procedimentos e processos executados fora do repositório estão fora do escopo, pois são inviáveis de extrair.

title: Detecting Sudden Drift for Single-Instance Log in Software Development Process
authors: Jiayi Yuan; Tong Li; Rui Zhu; Zifei Ma; Jiayuan Peng; Yue Huang
venue: 2020 Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Besançon)
journal_name: 2020 Prognostics and Health Management Conference (PHM-Besançon)
abstract: The current algorithm for detecting drift in the business process can only detect the drift of the log of multi-instance. However, the log generated in the software development process is mainly single-instance. Hence, this paper proposes an algorithm for detecting sudden drift of the log of single-instance. First, we apply the LDA topic model for the topic extraction of log in the software development process to obtain the log of single-instance. Then the location of the drift point of the single-instance log can be detected according to the change of the lowest frequency of direct successor relation. Finally, the location numbers of the actual drift points were obtained by corresponding to original data. Multiple sets of simulated data evaluate this algorithm. The results show that this algorithm can effectively detect the sudden drift location of the single-instance log in the software development process.
research_question: Como detectar mudanças abruptas (sudden drift) em logs de instância única gerados em processos de desenvolvimento de software, sem necessidade de particionamento prévio do log?
main_finding: O algoritmo proposto combina modelo de tópicos LDA com análise de frequência de relações diretas de sucessão para detectar efetivamente pontos de drift em logs de instância única de processos de software, operando no nível de atividade em vez de caso.
limitations: O trabalho foca apenas em detecção de sudden drift; a abordagem usa dados simulados para validação; limitações em relação a outros tipos de drift (gradual, recorrente, incremental) não são abordadas.

title: Unearthing open source decision-making processes: A case study of python enhancement proposals
authors: P. Sharma; B. Savarimuthu; N. Stanger
venue: Software, Practice & Experience
journal_name: Software: Practice and Experience
abstract: Good governance practices are pivotal to the success of Open Source Software (OSS) projects. However, the decision‐making processes that are made available to stakeholders are at times incomplete and may remain buried and hidden in large amounts of software repository data. This work bridges this gap by unearthing enacted decision‐making processes available for Python Enhancement Proposals (PEPs) from 1.54 million email messages that embody decisions made during the evolution of the Python language. This work employs a design science approach in operationalizing a framework called DeMaP miner that is used to discover hidden processes using information retrieval and information extraction techniques. It also uses process mining techniques to visualize the processes, and comparative structural analysis techniques to compare different decision processes. The work identifies a richer set of decision‐making activities than those reported on the Python website and in prior research work (48 new decision activities, 199 new pathways and 6 new stages). The extracted decision process has been positively evaluated by a prominent member of the Python steering council. The extracted process can be used for process compliance checking and process improvement in OSS communities. Additionally, the DeMaP Miner framework can be extended and customized to suit other OSS projects, such as the OpenJDK project.
pdf_file: Softw Pract Exp - 2022 - Nand Sharma - Unearthing open source decision%E2%80%90making processes  A case study of python enhancement.pdf
research_question: Quais são os processos de tomada de decisão realmente praticados nas propostas de melhoria do Python (PEPs) e como eles diferem dos processos descritos oficialmente?
tool_used: DeMaP Miner | information retrieval e information extraction techniques
main_finding: O estudo descobriu 48 novas atividades de decisão, 199 novos caminhos e 6 novos estágios não relatados anteriormente no site do Python ou em pesquisas anteriores. O framework DeMaP Miner foi desenvolvido para descobrir processos ocultos em dados de repositórios de software usando técnicas de mineração de processos e análise estrutural comparativa.
limitations: não_mencionado

title: Intervention of knowledge flows in software process mining projects
authors: Silvia Jaqueline Urrea-Contreras; María Angélica Astorga Vargas; B. L. F. Ríos; J. E. I. Esquer; F. F. González-Navarro
venue: Mexican International Conference on Computer Science
journal_name: 2022 IEEE Mexican International Conference on Computer Science (ENC)
abstract: Knowledge flows permit to analyze the type and sources of knowledge, and the way in which knowledge flows or is managed within an organization. This paper presents how a knowledge flow approach implemented in a process mining project, which analyzes process instances, supports the categorization of the different types and assets of knowledge stored in the repositories of software development organizations. In addition, it provides information for the roles involved in improvement cycles to make strategic decisions based on the types of mining (process discovery, conformance checking and process improvement). We present the implementation of the intervention methodology for the identification of processes, flows and types of knowledge with the objective of strengthening and validating the compliance of software processes. These results were validated with an experience report on the analysis of knowledge flows in a Mexican software development company implementing a Process Mining project.
research_question: Como os fluxos de conhecimento podem ser identificados e gerenciados em projetos de process mining de software para apoiar a tomada de decisões estratégicas e melhorias de processos?
main_finding: A implementação de uma abordagem de fluxos de conhecimento em projetos de process mining permite categorizar tipos e ativos de conhecimento armazenados em repositórios de organizações de desenvolvimento de software, fornecendo informações para que os papéis envolvidos tomem decisões estratégicas baseadas em descoberta de processos, verificação de conformidade e melhoria de processos.
limitations: O estudo foi validado em uma única empresa de desenvolvimento de software mexicana; a pesquisa não aborda especificamente técnicas estocásticas ou modelos probabilísticos.

title: A Data-Centric Approach to Evaluate Requirements Engineering in Multidisciplinary Projects
authors: A. Salmani; Aaron Imani; Majid Bahrehvar; L. Duffett-Leger; Mohammad Moshirpour
venue: IEEE International Conference on Systems, Man and Cybernetics
journal_name: 2022 IEEE International Conference on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics (SMC)
abstract: Multidisciplinary teams are often a necessity for software projects as they provide the required expertise to effectively solve complex problems. However, efficient collaboration between teams with different disciplines is challenging due to several factors such as considering gaps in knowledge areas, establishing a development process, and understanding different requirements from various groups or stakeholders. The agile methodology, such as scrum, offers a powerful approach to managing the software development process effectively. As part of the agile methodology, some techniques and tools are used to manage requirements change, which is a common practice in multidisciplinary teams. This research aims to leverage process-mining techniques to analyze data from Jira and GitHub to analyze the efficacy of software development process, particularly in multidisciplinary teams. This approach is applied to a case study of a virtual healthcare intervention system to measure the team’s productivity. The results indicate several deficiencies in the process with respect to requirements engineering task that cause loss of time and increase rework rates. Results indicate that there are some challenges in the development process that contribute to some deficiencies. The rework rate is high and the number of tasks that are intended to be completed is less than what was planned. These factors can contribute to the lengthening of the software development process. Most of these challenges can be addressed by improving the requirement engineering process in order to obtain the requirements and manage change requests more efficiently.
research_question: Como técnicas de process mining podem ser aplicadas para avaliar a eficácia do processo de engenharia de requisitos em equipes multidisciplinares de desenvolvimento de software?
main_finding: A análise de process mining sobre dados do Jira e GitHub revelou deficiências no processo de engenharia de requisitos em equipes multidisciplinares, incluindo alto índice de rework e falhas na conclusão de tarefas planejadas. Os principais desafios identificados foram falta de conhecimento de domínio compartilhado e comunicação inadequada entre profissionais de diferentes disciplinas.
limitations: O estudo foi conduzido em um único projeto de case study (Robin's Nest), limitando a generalização dos resultados. Não foram especificadas técnicas estatísticas de análise ou validação independente dos achados.

title: An AI-Enhanced Process Mining Framework for Software Process Insights
authors: Minh Khoi Nguyen; H. Tran; Razan Abualsaud; Ileana Ober
venue: International Conference on Knowledge and Systems Engineering
journal_name: 2023 15th International Conference on Knowledge and Systems Engineering (KSE)
abstract: Using process mining to gain insight of software processes presents several challenges, especially in preparing good-quality input data for the mining algorithm from the low-level data recorded by non-process-aware systems, such as version control systems. This paper proposes a process mining framework integrating AI techniques to enhance and simplify the execution of process mining tasks in this context. First, we implement a learning process to build an ontology capturing the knowledge of the analyzed processes' domain. Then this domain ontology is used in constructing an event log that enables an effective process discovery and analysis. The domain ontology plays the role of a domain expert in matching low-level events to high-abstraction and meaningful process activities. We implemented and experienced the proposed framework to extract process knowledge of e-commerce web applications development.
research_question: Como integrar técnicas de IA com process mining para melhorar a extração e preparação de event logs a partir de sistemas de controle de versão em processos de desenvolvimento de software?
main_finding: O framework proposto integra construção semi-automática de ontologia de domínio com técnicas de IA para filtrar e agrupar eventos de baixo nível extraídos de sistemas de controle de versão, associando-os a atividades significativas de processo. A abordagem resolve desafios críticos de extração e preparação de event logs em processos de software, exemplificado em aplicações web de e-commerce.
limitations: O paper apresenta apenas conceitos preliminares do framework sem resultados experimentais completos documentados nas páginas fornecidas.

title: Towards Streamlining Auditing for Compliance with Requirements in Open-Source Software at NASA
authors: Carlos Paradis; Ivan Perez; Misty D. Davies
venue: Symposium on Dependable Autonomic and Secure Computing
journal_name: 2025 AIAA DATC/IEEE 44th Digital Avionics Systems Conference (DASC)
abstract: Context: Software that operates in critical environments must be developed and maintained following strict software engineering and development processes. The instantiation of such processes may vary per project; however, once decided upon, projects must undergo audits to evaluate compliance with such requirements. Aim: We propose that audit effort can be reduced when requirements are realized by leveraging commonly used opensource infrastructure for version control, issue tracking and continuous integration, and the generated records are analyzed using a repository mining software tool to quantify process compliance. Method: We perform a case study in the NASA-funded Copilot project, utilizing Kaiaulu, a repository mining software tool. We define four software compliance metrics based on the Copilot's requirements, and analyze their impact on source code quality. Results: Our work demonstrates how it is possible to leverage existing open source tools and platforms to facilitate software certification and qualification, and to streamline the auditing process required even when stringent requirements must be enforced. Conclusion: Together, both project and tool can be utilized to visualize project compliance, and metrics can be defined to more easily identify process irregularities to minimize auditing efforts. Project Repository: github.com/Copilot-Language/copilot Tool Repository: github.com/sailuh/kaiaulu
research_question: Como reduzir o esforço de auditoria de conformidade de requisitos em software de código aberto da NASA através da análise automatizada de repositórios?
main_finding: O trabalho demonstra como estender ferramentas de análise de repositórios existentes para detectar conformidade com processos de desenvolvimento através de métricas específicas. A abordagem leverages infraestrutura padrão de código aberto (Git, GitHub, Travis CI) para facilitar auditoria de conformidade com requisitos NASA NPR7150.2, reduzindo esforço manual de compilação de evidências.
limitations: não_mencionado

title: MINDS: An NLP-Driven Process Mining Framework for Superior Microservices Decomposition
authors: Nassima Ait Mansour; H. Sbai; Karim Baïna
venue: IEEE Access
journal_name: IEEE Access
abstract: The decomposition of monolithic applications into microservice architectures is one of the most challenging tasks in modern software engineering, requiring architects to balance functional cohesion, minimize coupling, and preserve business logic integrity. Existing approaches rely on static analysis or single-dimensional criteria, failing to leverage business domain semantics and behavioral insights from system execution. This paper introduces the Microservices Identification through Natural language and Dynamic Sequence analysis (MINDS) framework, a novel approach that integrates inductive mining with natural language processing to achieve superior automated microservice decomposition. MINDS functions via a five-phase pipeline: input data are loaded by extracting user traces using our algorithm to build an event log. Natural Language Processing (NLP) preprocessing employs semantic classification to discern business domain concepts and categorize activities based on functional relationships; inductive mining derives behavioral models from event logs within each domain cluster; post-processing examines process trees to pinpoint optimal decomposition points according to structural complexity and quality metrics; and documentation generation creates detailed microservice specifications encompassing Business Process Model and Notation (BPMN) models and Application Programming Interface (API) definitions. We evaluated MINDS for four applications using event logs with 6,000 activities each. MINDS effectively outperformed the prominent approaches for all the quality parameters. The cohesion scores, coupling metrics, and process mining quality metrics exceeded the thresholds for fitness and precision scores. MINDS enhances automated microservices decomposition through methodological innovation and superior empirical performance. The combination of process mining and natural language processing facilitates the discovery of service boundaries that align with the execution patterns and business domain concepts.
research_question: Como integrar process mining com processamento de linguagem natural para alcançar decomposição superior de aplicações monolíticas em microserviços, considerando tanto padrões comportamentais quanto semântica de domínio?
main_finding: O framework MINDS combina inductive mining com NLP para identificar limites de microserviços alinhados com padrões de execução e conceitos de domínio. Alcançou scores de coesão de 78,2%-80,6% e métricas de acoplamento de 19,1%-21,7%, superando significativamente abordagens existentes. A geração automatizada de especificações produção-ready atinge scores de 91,3%-96,7%.
limitations: Avaliação realizada em apenas quatro aplicações com event logs de 6.000 atividades cada. Não há discussão detalhada sobre escalabilidade para sistemas ainda maiores ou variabilidade em tipos de aplicações monolíticas.

title: Comparative Analysis of AI Software and Traditional Software GitHub Repositories Using Process Mining
authors: Oguzhan Tasci; T. Erdogan
venue: Conference on Computer Science and Information Systems
journal_name: 2025 20th Conference on Computer Science and Intelligence Systems (FedCSIS)
abstract: This study conducts a comparative analysis of Artificial Intelligence (AI) software and traditional GitHub repositories, focusing on workflow efficiency and sentiment dynamics. Using process mining and sentiment analysis techniques, we examine repositories from eight prominent projects, encompassing diverse datasets of issues and pull requests filtered for relevance and consistency. Our findings reveal that AI software repositories exhibit different workflow patterns and sentiment dynamics compared to traditional repositories. Sentiment analysis uncovers that contributors to AI software repositories experience more positive sentiment dynamics, likely reflecting structured workflows and collaborative tools. Conversely, traditional repositories exhibit longer resolution times and more fluctuating sentiment patterns, which may indicate higher complexity or less automation. These insights provide valuable recommendations for optimizing repository management, fostering contributor satisfaction, and improving collaborative software development environments.
research_question: Quais são as diferenças nos padrões de fluxo de trabalho e dinâmica de sentimento entre repositórios de software com IA e software tradicional no GitHub?
main_finding: Repositórios de software com IA apresentam padrões de fluxo de trabalho mais estruturados e dinâmica de sentimento mais positiva comparado a repositórios tradicionais. Repositórios tradicionais exibem tempos de resolução mais longos e padrões de sentimento mais flutuantes, indicando maior complexidade ou menor automação.
limitations: O estudo não menciona explicitamente limitações no texto fornecido.

title: Analysis of software fault removal policies using a non-homogeneous continuous time Markov chain
authors: S. Gokhale; Michael R. Lyu; Kishor S. Trivedi
venue: Software quality journal
journal_name: Software Quality Journal
research_question: Como diferentes políticas de remoção de falhas afetam o número residual de falhas em software durante o processo de teste, considerando o tempo não-negligenciável de remoção e imperfeições no processo?
main_finding: O artigo apresenta um framework baseado em Cadeias de Markov de Tempo Contínuo Não-Homogêneas (NHCTMC) que incorpora explicitamente o tempo de remoção de falhas e imperfeições no processo de remoção. O método proposto permite analisar diferentes estratégias de remoção de falhas e computar a intensidade de falhas do software na presença de remoção de falhas explícita.
limitations: O modelo assume inicialmente taxa de remoção constante, posteriormente estendida para dependências de tempo e estado. As soluções envolvem métodos numéricos ao invés de expressões em forma fechada.

title: Modeling of software development process with the Markov processes
authors: T. Savchuk; N. Pryimak
journal_name: Eastern-European Journal of Enterprise Technologies
abstract: The comparative analysis of the existing research on the application of formal approaches to the software development process modeling is performed. Based on the analysis, the urgency of modeling of the software development process as a Markov random process is substantiated. An information model of association rule mining and application in software development is developed. The information model represents the process and can be used in the design of appropriate information technology. The research, which determined the number of steps needed to develop one software component and the whole software is carried out. The levels of detail of the software development process such as the level, representing the development of software, which is a finite set of software components; the level, representing a detailed description of the stages of development of a particular component; the level, representing a detailed description a certain stage of development of a particular component are identified. For each level, the relevant stages of software development are described. Modeling of the software development process with the Markov chains is conducted. This will allow using a single mathematical tool to represent the corresponding process at different levels of detail
research_question: Como modelar o processo de desenvolvimento de software usando cadeias de Markov em diferentes níveis de detalhamento? Como integrar a busca de regras associativas no desenvolvimento de software através de processos estocásticos?
main_finding: Os autores propõem um modelo de informação baseado em cadeias de Markov para formalizar o processo de desenvolvimento de software, permitindo modelagem em diferentes níveis de detalhe. O trabalho apresenta abordagens formais para descrever dinamicamente o processo de desenvolvimento, integrando conceitos de regras associativas.
limitations: O texto disponível não fornece informações completas sobre limitações reportadas, avaliação experimental ou validação do modelo proposto.

title: Transforming Cognitive Maps into Markov Process Models for Software Development Projects
authors: Kateryna Kolesnikova; Alua Myrzakerimova; Nursultan Alpysbay; Tetiana Olekh
venue: 2023 IEEE International Conference on Smart Information Systems and Technologies (SIST)
journal_name: 2023 IEEE International Conference on Smart Information Systems and Technologies (SIST)
abstract: The complexity of managing software development projects in organizational and technical systems is due to a number of features. Such projects are weakly structured systems. The parameters of weakly structured systems form a complex “web” of connections and states that change over time. The development of projects in such a multifactorial environment can only be represented in the form of qualitative models. The use of cognitive maps with their subsequent presentation in the form of Markov chains allows quantifying the course of project processes, which are the main condition for the success of the project. At the same time, quantitative estimates represent not only a multi-vector picture of the state of projects, but also have the property of forecasting. The scope of the proposed method can be significantly expanded through the use in the educational process in the training of professionals in the field of programming and project management.
research_question: Como transformar mapas cognitivos em modelos de cadeias de Markov para melhorar a gestão e previsão de estados em projetos de desenvolvimento de software?
main_finding: O método proposto integra mapas cognitivos com cadeias de Markov para quantificar processos de projetos de software, fornecendo uma representação multi-vetorial do estado dos projetos com propriedades de previsão. A abordagem permite gerenciar sistemas fracamente estruturados através de modelos qualitativos transformados em estimativas quantitativas.
limitations: não_mencionado

title: Mathematical model of the software development process according to the 'outsource' scheme
authors: Serhii Hlushko; Oleksii Leunenko; Ivan Mikhieiev
venue: International Conference on Dependable Systems, Services and Technologies
journal_name: 2023 13th International Conference on Dependable Systems, Services and Technologies (DESSERT)
abstract: The software development process is complex and multifaceted, influenced by a multitude of factors such as project complexity, collaboration among different teams, interaction with clients, and development methodologies. Efficiently managing this process becomes a crucial task for ensuring quality and meeting deadlines. In the context of the “outsourcing” scheme, where certain stages of software product development can be delegated to external specialists or teams, challenges arise in assessing the probabilistic-temporal characteristics of the development process. Questions arise about the expected time for each stage's completion, the likelihood of meeting set deadlines, and the factors exerting the greatest impact on process effectiveness. The objective of this research is to develop a mathematical model that enables the analysis of probabilistic-temporal characteristics of software development processes within the “outsourcing” scheme. The model takes into account various factors, including participants' expertise level, communication effectiveness, and other parameters influencing stage durations. The proposed mathematical model incorporates probabilistic transitions between stages, with transition probabilities and intensities depending on the involvement of external specialists and communication effectiveness. Relevant probabilistic distributions are used to estimate the duration of each stage, and statistical methods are employed to analyze probabilistic outcomes. By considering the intricacies of the “outsourcing” scheme, this study aims to facilitate informed decision-making in resource allocation, project planning, and risk assessment within the domain of software development. Insights derived from the model's probabilistic-temporal characteristics provide valuable tools for enhancing process management and outcomes in the dynamic realm of software development.
research_question: Como desenvolver um modelo matemático que analise características probabilístico-temporais de processos de desenvolvimento de software sob o esquema de outsourcing, considerando fatores como expertise dos participantes e efetividade de comunicação?
main_finding: O modelo matemático proposto incorpora transições probabilísticas entre estágios de desenvolvimento, com probabilidades e intensidades de transição dependendo do envolvimento de especialistas externos e efetividade de comunicação. O modelo utiliza distribuições probabilísticas relevantes para estimar a duração de cada estágio, permitindo análise de características probabilístico-temporais em processos de outsourcing.
limitations: O trabalho não analisa formalmente processos reais de desenvolvimento de software e carece de validação empírica. A pesquisa não é direcionada à formalização de metodologias específicas de incorporação dos fatores identificados.

title: A structured stochastic model for software project estimation in Waterfall models
authors: Ildo Massitela; Joaquim Assunção; Alan R. Santos; Paulo Fernandes
venue: International Conference on Software Engineering and Knowledge Engineering
abstract: Evaluate team’s performance on long-duration projects can be a challenge. It relies on human expertise to perform estimations, which can generate uncertainty and dependency of experienced specialists. Statistical techniques, such as modeling and simulations, are suitable options as tools to support projects estimations. Our goal in this paper is a formal mapping of the main Waterfall model characteristics to stochastic model to predict performance indices of teams such as the real working time.
research_question: Como modelar formalmente o desempenho de equipes em projetos Waterfall de longa duração usando técnicas estocásticas para melhorar a estimação de recursos e tempo?
main_finding: O modelo estocástico estruturado baseado em Stochastic Automata Networks (SAN) permite representar e simular o desempenho de equipes Waterfall considerando expertise variada, estados de trabalho, colaboração e retrabalho, fornecendo estimativas mais confiáveis que dependem apenas de expertise humana.
limitations: O modelo foi validado com dados de apenas um projeto industrial específico (Project X) e os autores reconhecem que não se trata de uma solução completa.

title: Constructing a Hybrid Software Process Simulation Model in Practice: An Exemplar from Industry
authors: Yue Li; He Zhang; Liming Dong; Bohan Liu; Jinyu Ma
venue: International Conference on Software and Systems Process
journal_name: 2020 IEEE/ACM International Conference on Software and System Processes (ICSSP)
abstract: Background: Software Process Simulation Modeling (SPSM) is of paramount importance to support quantitative management of software development process. Hybrid process simulation combines multiple simulation paradigms to reflect complex changes in realistic software processes, which brings inherent challenges to process management. Constructing a hybrid model requires more modeling expertise and experience than modeling by solo-paradigm. However, a few studies explicitly discuss the challenges they encountered as a topic, which may discourage practitioners.Objective: Our aim in this study is to present an industrial process modeling project as an exemplar to demonstrate and discuss the technical issues and challenges associated with hybrid process simulation in practice.Method: Based on the collaboration with a global software enterprise, we constructed a hybrid process simulation model that combines System Dynamics (SD) and Discrete Event Simulation (DES) to predict the project duration and release date for management.Results: Several challenges around hybrid process simulation of software development process are identified and discussed with the proposal of sets of solutions from different perspectives. The model is validated by comparing the simulation result with the actual enactment of the process in industry. In addition, the result confirms the rationality and efficacy of the suggested solutions to some extent.Conclusions: In the collaboration with the enterprise, five-step modeling procedure was adopted for constructing the hybrid process model. The experience reported about the detailed steps of hybrid modeling may offer reference value to the SPSM community.
research_question: Quais são os desafios técnicos e as soluções práticas para construir modelos de simulação de processos de software híbridos que combinem múltiplos paradigmas de simulação?
main_finding: O estudo apresenta um modelo de simulação híbrido que combina System Dynamics e Discrete Event Simulation para prever duração de projetos e datas de release em uma empresa de software global. Identifica e discute cinco desafios principais e propõe soluções práticas, validando o modelo através de comparação com dados reais de enactment do processo.
limitations: O estudo é baseado em um único caso industrial, o que limita a generalização dos resultados. Não há descrição detalhada sobre como as ferramentas de simulação específicas foram utilizadas ou quais parâmetros estocásticos foram configurados.

title: DevOps workflow verification and duration prediction using non-Markovian stochastic Petri nets
authors: Walid Ben Mesmia; M. Escheikh; Kamel Barkaoui
venue: J. Softw. Evol. Process.
journal_name: Journal of Software: Evolution and Process
abstract: In this paper, we provide a non‐Markovian Stochastic Petri Net (SPN) model for DevOps workflow specification, and we determine how business processes are carried out. After describing our model semantics, we show how general properties related to liveness and safety can be checked. After that, we provide several extensions on SPNs (SPN) the notation and expressivity to check some specific properties related to actors' (Developers and Operators) availability, interactions between the actors, and execution failures detection linked to the DevOps steps. Next, we validate the proposed model relevance with MATLAB simulation through a specific DevOps case study. Finally, we propose a truncated density function to anticipate the delays related to the DevOps business process overall steps.
research_question: Como especificar, verificar e prever a duração de workflows DevOps usando redes de Petri estocásticas não-Markovianas?
main_finding: Propõe um modelo de Rede de Petri Estocástica não-Markoviana (NM-WSPN) para especificar workflows DevOps e verifica propriedades de liveness e safety. Valida o modelo através de simulação MATLAB e propõe uma função de densidade truncada para antecipar atrasos nos processos DevOps.
limitations: não_mencionado

title: Assessing the risk of software development in agile methodologies using simulation
authors: M. I. Lunesu; R. Tonelli; Lodovica Marchesi; M. Marchesi
venue: IEEE Access
journal_name: IEEE Access
abstract: Agile methodologies aim to reduce software development risk using short iterations, feature-driven development, continuous integration, testing automation, and other practices. However, the risk of project failure or time and budget overruns is still a relevant problem. This paper aims to present and discuss a new approach to model some key risk factors in agile development, using software process simulation modeling (SPSM), which can complement other approaches, and whose usage is particularly suited for agile development. We introduce a new approach to modeling some key risk factors – namely project duration, number of implemented issues, and key statistics of issue completion time – using a simulator of agile development, which we developed for this purpose. The approach includes modeling the agile process, gathering data from the tool used for project management, and performing Monte Carlo simulations of the process, to get insights about the expected time and effort to complete the project, and about their distributions. The model’s parameters that can cause risk are errors in effort estimation of the features to develop, variations in developers’ assignment to these features, impediments related to developers’ availability and work completion. To validate the simulator, and to demonstrate how the method can be used, we analyzed three open-source projects, gathering their data from JIRA repositories. We ran Monte Carlo simulations of these projects, showing that the simulator can well approximate the progress of the real project, then varying the identified risk factors and statistically evaluating their effects on the risk parameters. The proposed approach is relevant for project managers, being able to quantitatively evaluate the risks, provided that the process and the project’s data are properly modeled and gathered. As for the future, we are working to improve our risk assessment method, evaluating it on more case studies, scaling the model from a single team to multiple teams involved in one or more projects.
research_question: Em que medida é possível automatizar e modelar fatores-chave de risco em desenvolvimento ágil usando simulação de processos de software? Como os fatores de risco afetam a duração do projeto, número de issues implementadas e distribuição de tempo de conclusão?
main_finding: O simulador desenvolvido consegue aproximar bem o progresso real do projeto através de simulações Monte Carlo, permitindo avaliar quantitativamente os riscos. Os principais fatores de risco identificados são: erros na estimativa de esforço das features, variações na alocação de desenvolvedores, impedimentos relacionados à disponibilidade dos desenvolvedores e ao tempo de conclusão do trabalho.
limitations: Validação realizada em apenas três projetos open-source; modelo limitado a equipes únicas; necessidade de melhoria na avaliação com mais casos de estudo e escalabilidade para múltiplas equipes.

title: A Stochastic Petri net Model of Continuous Integration and Continuous Delivery
authors: Sushovan Bhadra
venue: 2022 IEEE International Symposium on Software Reliability Engineering Workshops (ISSREW)
journal_name: 2022 IEEE International Symposium on Software Reliability Engineering Workshops (ISSREW)
abstract: Modern software development organizations rely on continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD), since it allows developers to continuously integrate their code in a single shared repository and automates the delivery process of the product to the user. While modern software practices improve the performance of the software life cycle, they also increase the complexity of this process. Past studies make improvements to the performance of the CI/CD pipeline. However, there are fewer formal models to quantitatively guide process and product quality improvement or characterize how automated and human activities compose and interact asynchronously. Therefore, this paper develops a Petri net model to analyze a CI/CD pipeline to improve process performance in terms of the probability of successfully delivering new or updated functionality by a specified deadline. The utility of the model is demonstrated through a sensitivity analysis to identify stages of the pipeline where improvements would most significantly improve the probability of timely product delivery.
research_question: Como modelar formalmente um pipeline de CI/CD usando Petri nets estocásticas para analisar e melhorar o desempenho do processo, particularmente a probabilidade de entregar funcionalidades com sucesso dentro de prazos especificados?
main_finding: O estudo desenvolve um modelo de Petri net estocástica para analisar pipelines de CI/CD, permitindo quantificar o impacto de melhorias em diferentes estágios do pipeline. A análise de sensibilidade identifica quais estágios do pipeline proporcionariam melhorias mais significativas na probabilidade de entrega oportuna do produto.
limitations: O paper não menciona explicitamente as limitações do estudo nas primeiras páginas fornecidas. O modelo proposto carece de validação empírica com dados reais de pipelines de CI/CD em larga escala.

title: A stochastic petri net model of continuous integration and continuous delivery
authors: Sushovan Bhadra; B. Das; Priscila Silva; V. Nagaraju; L. Fiondella
venue: Reliability and Maintainability Symposium
journal_name: 2023 Annual Reliability and Maintainability Symposium (RAMS)
abstract: SUMMARY & CONCLUSIONSModern software development organizations rely on continuous integration and continuous delivery (CI/CD), since it allows developers to continuously integrate their code in a single shared repository and automates the delivery process of the product to the user. While modern software practices improve the performance of the software life cycle, they also increase the complexity of this process. Past studies make improvements to the performance of the CI/CD pipeline. However, there are fewer formal models to quantitatively guide process and product quality improvement or characterize how automated and human activities compose and interact asynchronously. Therefore, this paper develops a stochastic Petri net model to analyze a CI/CD pipeline to improve process performance in terms of the probability of successfully delivering new or updated functionality by a specified deadline. The utility of the model is demonstrated through a sensitivity analysis to identify stages of the pipeline where improvements would most significantly improve the probability of timely product delivery.
research_question: Como modelar formalmente um pipeline de CI/CD usando Petri nets estocásticas para melhorar a probabilidade de entrega bem-sucedida de funcionalidades dentro de um prazo especificado? Como identificar os estágios do pipeline onde melhorias teriam maior impacto no desempenho?
main_finding: O artigo desenvolve um modelo de Petri net estocástica para analisar pipelines de CI/CD, caracterizando como atividades automatizadas e humanas se compõem e interagem assincronamente. Através de análise de sensibilidade, identifica-se os estágios críticos do pipeline onde melhorias resultariam em maior probabilidade de entrega bem-sucedida de produtos dentro de prazos especificados.
limitations: Não mencionadas nas primeiras páginas do artigo. O trabalho busca compreender atividades comuns em pipelines CI/CD de grande escala em repositórios open source, mas metodologia e datasets específicos não são descritos nas seções apresentadas.

title: Comparative Study of Reinforcement Learning in GitHub Pull Request Outcome Predictions
authors: Rinkesh Joshi; N. Kahani
venue: IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution, and Reengineering
journal_name: 2024 IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution and Reengineering (SANER)
abstract: In the rapidly evolving field of software development, pull-based development models, facilitated by tools such as GitHub, are essential for collaboration. This study explores factors that influence pull request (PR) outcomes and employs two Reinforcement Learning (RL) formalizations, modeled as Markov Decision Processes, for PR outcome prediction. The first model leverages 72 PR features and achieves a G-mean score of 0.82664, while the second focuses solely on PR discussions, resulting in a G-mean of 0.88372. Using a specially designed reward function, these RL formalizations strategically address data imbalance and excel in mimicking both single-stage and multi-stage PR review processes. They outperform baseline models (Random Forest, X G Boost, and a Naive Bayes baseline) across various data splits-namely 80/20, 50/50, and 20/80-and are particularly effective at predicting PR rejections. The study also makes its datasets publicly available for future research.
research_question: Como abordagens de Reinforcement Learning são viáveis e eficazes para prever resultados de revisões de Pull Requests no GitHub? Como essas abordagens se comparam com modelos baseline e existentes?
main_finding: Dois modelos RL baseados em Processos de Decisão de Markov foram desenvolvidos: um com 72 features PR alcançando G-mean de 0.82664 e outro focado em discussões PR com G-mean de 0.88372. Os modelos RL superam baselines (Random Forest, XGBoost, Naive Bayes) em múltiplos splits de dados e são especialmente eficazes em prever rejeições de PR.
limitations: não_mencionado

title: Fault-insertion and fault-fixing behavioural patterns in Apache Software Foundation Projects
authors: Marco Ortu; Giuseppe Destefanis; T. Hall; David Bowes
venue: Information and Software Technology
journal_name: Inf. Softw. Technol.
research_question: Como os padrões de inserção e correção de falhas variam entre desenvolvedores em projetos de software? Desenvolvedores com expertise em tópicos de código inserem menos falhas e corrigem mais falhas?
tool_used: LDA (Latent Dirichlet Allocation) | Network Analysis | Petri-net | Markov Chain | Hawkes Processes
main_finding: A maioria dos projetos analisados possui desenvolvedores que dominam a inserção e correção de falhas. Falhas são menos prováveis de serem inseridas por desenvolvedores com expertise em tópicos de código. Diferentes projetos apresentam padrões distintos de inserção e correção de falhas ao longo do tempo.
limitations: não_mencionado

title: Exploring Collaboration Patterns in GitHub Using Discrete Time Markov Chain
authors: Suhee Jo; Ryeonggu Kwon; Gihwon Kwon
venue: Asia-Pacific Software Engineering Conference
journal_name: 2023 30th Asia-Pacific Software Engineering Conference (APSEC)
abstract: Collaboration among team members plays a crucial role in the success of software projects. GitHub has emerged as a prominent platform for collaborative software development. This study focuses on analyzing GitHub repositories to gain insights into project status and participant characteristics based on development activities such as commits and pull requests. Utilizing a discrete time Markov chain, this research models GitHub repositories and employs probabilistic temporal logic and model checking to shed light on the activity levels of both projects and participants. We expect that understanding these observed trends and patterns can assist project managers in strengthening teamwork and fostering productive collaboration.
research_question: Quais são os padrões de colaboração e atividades de desenvolvimento em repositórios GitHub e como modelá-los usando Discrete Time Markov Chain para melhorar o gerenciamento de projetos?
main_finding: O estudo modela atividades de desenvolvimento em repositórios GitHub usando DTMC, identificando que commits representam 62.73% das atividades de longo prazo. A abordagem utiliza model checking com lógica temporal probabilística para extrair padrões de colaboração que podem auxiliar gerentes de projeto no fortalecimento do trabalho em equipe.
limitations: Análise limitada a 30 repositórios pequenos (3-10 contribuidores) criados em 2022 com pelo menos 100 commits, o que pode não representar projetos de maior escala ou diferentes contextos de desenvolvimento.

title: Probabilistic Model Checking GitHub Repositories for Software Project Analysis
authors: Suhee Jo; Ryeonggu Kwon; Gihwon Kwon
venue: Applied Sciences
journal_name: Applied Sciences
abstract: GitHub serves as a platform for collaborative software development, where contributors engage, evolve projects, and shape the community. This study presents a novel approach to analyzing GitHub activity that departs from traditional methods. Using Discrete-Time Markov Chains and probabilistic Computation Tree Logic for model checking, we aim to uncover temporal dynamics, probabilities, and key factors influencing project behavior. By explicitly modeling state transitions, our approach provides transparency and explainability for sequential properties. The application of our method to five repositories demonstrates its feasibility and scalability and provides insights into the long-term probabilities of various activities. In particular, the analysis provides valuable perspectives for project managers to optimize team dynamics and resource allocation. The query specifications developed for model checking allow users to generate and execute queries for specific aspects, demonstrating scalability beyond the queries we present. In conclusion, our analysis provides an understanding of GitHub repository properties, branch management, and subscriber behavior. We anticipate its applicability to various open-source projects, revealing trends among contributors based on the unique characteristics of repositories.
research_question: Como usar Cadeias de Markov em Tempo Discreto e Model Checking Probabilístico para analisar padrões de atividades em repositórios GitHub e descobrir dinâmicas temporais que influenciam o comportamento de projetos de software?
main_finding: A aplicação de DTMC e Computation Tree Logic probabilística em cinco repositórios GitHub revelou dinâmicas temporais, probabilidades de transição entre estados e fatores-chave que influenciam o comportamento do projeto. A abordagem fornece transparência e explicabilidade para propriedades sequenciais, oferecendo perspectivas valiosas para gerentes de projeto otimizarem dinâmicas de equipe e alocação de recursos.
limitations: O estudo foi aplicado a apenas cinco repositórios GitHub, e a generalização dos resultados para diferentes tipos de projetos de código aberto não é discutida em detalhes nas primeiras páginas.

title: An analysis of Monte Carlo simulations for forecasting software projects
authors: P. Miranda; J. Faria; F. F. Correia; Ahmed A. Fares; Ricardo Graça; João Mendes-Moreira
venue: ACM Symposium on Applied Computing
journal_name: Proceedings of the 36th Annual ACM Symposium on Applied Computing
abstract: Forecasts of the effort or delivery date can play an important role in managing software projects, but the estimates provided by development teams are often inaccurate and time-consuming to produce. This is not surprising given the uncertainty that underlies this activity. This work studies the use of Monte Carlo simulations for generating forecasts based on project historical data. We have designed and run experiments comparing these forecasts against what happened in practice and to estimates provided by developers, when available. Comparisons were made based on the mean magnitude of relative error (MMRE). We did also analyze how the forecasting accuracy varies with the amount of work to be forecasted and the amount of historical data used. To minimize the requirements on input data, delivery date forecasts for a set of user stories were computed based on takt time of past stories (time elapsed between the completion of consecutive stories); effort forecasts were computed based on full-time equivalent (FTE) hours allocated to the implementation of past stories. The MMRE of delivery date forecasting was 32% in a set of 10 runs (for different projects) of Monte Carlo simulation based on takt time. The MMRE of effort forecasting was 20% in a set of 5 runs of Monte Carlo simulation based on FTE allocation, much smaller than the MMRE of 134% of developers' estimates. A better forecasting accuracy was obtained when the number of historical data points was 20 or higher. These results suggest that Monte Carlo simulations may be used in practice for delivery date and effort forecasting in agile projects, after a few initial sprints.
research_question: Como simulações de Monte Carlo podem ser usadas para gerar previsões precisas de datas de entrega e esforço em projetos de software ágeis, e como a precisão varia com a quantidade de dados históricos disponíveis?
main_finding: Simulações de Monte Carlo baseadas em takt time alcançaram MMRE de 32% para previsão de datas de entrega e 20% para previsão de esforço (comparado com 134% de estimativas de desenvolvedores), demonstrando viabilidade prática. A precisão das previsões melhorou significativamente quando o número de pontos de dados históricos era 20 ou superior.
limitations: O estudo foi conduzido em um conjunto relativamente pequeno de execuções (10 para datas de entrega, 5 para esforço) e a técnica requer acúmulo inicial de dados históricos de vários sprints antes de ser aplicável.

title: Mining projects from structured and unstructured data
research_question: Como melhor apoiar gerentes de projeto na compreensão do projeto conforme executado, utilizando evidências de artefatos gerados, especialmente para fins de auditoria e verificação de conformidade em projetos críticos de segurança?
main_finding: O trabalho propõe uma abordagem para minerar projetos orientados a processos a partir de dados estruturados e não estruturados, integrando técnicas de process mining e text mining para extrair informações de repositórios de versão e listas de discussão, visando apoiar auditoria e conformidade em projetos de segurança crítica.
limitations: O trabalho encontra-se em estágio preliminar de pesquisa de doutorado, com resultados ainda não completamente disponíveis no documento. As principais limitações incluem a natureza ad-hoc dos projetos e a falta de dados estruturados de execução típicos de process mining.

title: Mining encrypted software logs using alpha algorithm
authors: Gamze Tillem; Z. Erkin; R. Lagendijk
venue: International Conference on Security and Cryptography
abstract: : The growing complexity of software with respect to technological advances encourages model-based analysis of software systems for validation and veriﬁcation. Process mining is one recently investigated technique for such analysis which enables the discovery of process models from event logs collected during software execution. However, the usage of logs in process mining can be harmful to the privacy of data owners. While for a software user the existence of sensitive information in logs can be a concern, for a software company, the intellectual property of their product and conﬁdential company information within logs can pose a threat to company’s privacy. In this paper, we propose a privacy-preserving protocol for the discovery of process models for software analysis that assures the privacy of users and companies. For this purpose, our proposal uses encrypted logs and processes them using cryptographic protocols in a two-party setting. Furthermore, our proposal applies data packing on the cryptographic protocols to optimize computations by reducing the number of repetitive operations. The experiments show that using data packing the performance of our protocol is promising for privacy-preserving software analysis. To the best of our knowledge, our protocol is the ﬁrst of its kind for the software analysis which relies on processing of encrypted logs using process mining techniques.
research_question: Como descobrir modelos de processo a partir de logs de software criptografados, preservando a privacidade dos usuários e empresas? É possível aplicar técnicas de process mining em dados encriptados mantendo a segurança?
main_finding: Proposta de um protocolo criptográfico inovador para descoberta de modelos de processo em logs encriptados usando o algoritmo alpha em contexto multi-party. O uso de data packing otimiza significativamente o desempenho computacional, tornando a análise privada de software viável. Este é o primeiro protocolo que combina process mining com processamento de logs encriptados para análise de software.
limitations: O estudo utiliza datasets sintéticos e não apresenta validação em cenários industriais reais. A escalabilidade para logs de grande volume e a aplicabilidade prática em sistemas de produção não são completamente investigadas.

title: Software process analysis methodology-a methodology based on lessons learned in embracing legacy software
authors: M. Leemans; Wil M.P. van der Aalst; M. Brand; R. Schiffelers; L. Lensink
venue: IEEE International Conference on Software Maintenance and Evolution
journal_name: 2018 IEEE International Conference on Software Maintenance and Evolution (ICSME)
abstract: Over the last decades, the complexity of high-tech systems, and the software systems controlling them, has increased considerably. In practice, it is hard to keep knowledge and documentation of these ever-evolving software systems up-to-date with their actual realization; we are dealing with legacy software. Clearly, this lack of knowledge, insight, and understanding is more and more becoming a critical issue. Process mining provides an interesting opportunity to improve understanding and analyze software behavior based on observations from the system on the run. However, a concrete software process analysis methodology was lacking. This paper 1) discusses a software process analysis case study at ASML, a large high-tech company, and, based on the lessons learned, 2) presents a concrete methodology for analyzing software processes. The presented methodology actively includes the system under analysis and is based on practical experiences in applying process mining on industrial-scale legacy software.
research_question: Como aplicar process mining para analisar e compreender o comportamento de sistemas de software legado em ambientes industriais de alta complexidade?
main_finding: Uma metodologia concreta de análise de processos de software baseada em lições aprendidas da aplicação de process mining em software legado industrial é apresentada. A metodologia inclui ativamente o sistema sob análise e demonstra a viabilidade prática de process mining em sistemas de alta complexidade e escala industrial.
limitations: O estudo é baseado em um único caso de estudo em uma grande empresa de alta tecnologia, limitando a generalização dos resultados. A disponibilidade de dados e a complexidade dos sistemas legados podem afetar a aplicabilidade em outros contextos.

title: Hierarchical performance analysis for process mining

limitations: não_mencionado

title: Automatic Discovery of Behavioral Models From Software Execution Data
authors: Cong Liu
venue: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
journal_name: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: During the execution of a software system, tremendous amounts of data are recorded, and such data provide valuable information on software runtime behavior analysis. This paper presents an approach on how to utilize process mining as an enabler to discover software behavioral models. To achieve this, we formally define the software event log and its transformation from original software execution data. Essentially, a software event log consists of a set of cases that each is a manifestation of an independent software run. A case is represented as an ordered sequence of events that each refers to a method call. Given the observation that software usually has a hierarchical structure, we first propose an approach to construct a hierarchical software event log from the original flat one by the recursively applying method calling relation detection. Next, using extended process discovery techniques, we discover a software behavioral model, which is represented as a kind of hierarchical Petri net with components, from the hierarchical software event log. We have implemented the proposed approach in the open source process mining toolkit ProM. By using two synthetic software event logs, we show that our approach can deal with infrequent behavior. Moreover, a validation with one real-life software event log shows that our approach can help visualize actual software runtime behavior in an easy-to-understand manner. Note to Practitioners—Software data analysis techniques play an increasingly important role for understanding the real behavior of software systems. This paper addresses the issue of discovering behavioral models from real-life software execution data. A kind of hierarchical Petri nets is defined to represent the discovered software behavior. All proposed concepts and algorithms are supported by open source tools, and experiments over both synthetic and real-life software execution data have shown their applicability. The proposed methodology is readily applicable to industrial-size software behavior discovery problems.
research_question: Como utilizar process mining para descobrir automaticamente modelos comportamentais de software a partir de dados de execução? Como representar hierarquicamente o comportamento de software em Petri nets?
main_finding: O artigo propõe uma abordagem de descoberta automática de modelos comportamentais de software utilizando process mining e Petri nets hierárquicas. A metodologia transforma dados brutos de execução em event logs estruturados hierarquicamente e descobre modelos que representam o comportamento real do software em tempo de execução. O enfoque em estruturas hierárquicas reflete a natureza hierarchical dos sistemas de software.
limitations: As validações foram limitadas a event logs sintéticos e um único event log real. A escalabilidade para sistemas grandes não foi extensivamente avaliada.

title: Application of process mining in project management context: An exploratory case study in software development projects
research_question: Como técnicas de process mining podem ser aplicadas para melhorar o gerenciamento de projetos em contextos de desenvolvimento de software?

limitations: Estudo exploratório limitado a um caso específico, sem generalização ampla dos resultados para outras contextos de desenvolvimento de software.

title: The evolutionary composition of desirable execution traces from event logs
authors: Asef Pourmasoumi; M. Kahani; E. Bagheri
venue: Future generations computer systems
journal_name: Future Gener. Comput. Syst.
abstract: Abstract In this paper, we propose an evolutionary computing approach based on Genetic Algorithms for composing an efficient trace given a desirable utility function based on the observations made in the event logs of several peer-organizations. Our proposed approach works with a set of event logs from different peer-organizations and generates an efficient trace according to a utility function. The main advantage of our approach is that we primarily work with event logs that are more accurate representations of the actual execution of a process within an organization. Furthermore, we generate efficient traces that are put together through the identification of sub-parts of the observed traces that are locally optimal. We report on our experiments on the BPIC’15 dataset that show improvement in terms of the optimality of the generated traces.
research_question: Como compor traces eficientes a partir de logs de eventos de múltiplas organizações peer usando uma função de utilidade desejável? Que abordagem baseada em computação evolutiva pode gerar traces otimizados identificando sub-partes localmente ótimas?
main_finding: A abordagem proposta baseada em Algoritmos Genéticos compõe traces eficientes através da composição de sub-partes otimizadas localmente extraídas de logs de eventos de organizações peer. Experimentos no dataset BPIC'15 demonstram melhoria em termos de optimalidade dos traces gerados em comparação com abordagens convencionais.
limitations: não_mencionado

title: Learning analytics to improve coding abilities: a fuzzy-based process mining approach
authors: P. Ardimento; M. Bernardi; Marta Cimitile; G. D. Ruvo
venue: IEEE International Conference on Fuzzy Systems
journal_name: 2019 IEEE International Conference on Fuzzy Systems (FUZZ-IEEE)
abstract: Comprehension of how students and developers head the development of software and what specific hurdles they face, have a strong potential to better support the coding workflow. In this paper, we present the CodingMiner environment to generate event logs from IDE usage enabling the adoption of fuzzy-based process mining techniques to model and to study the developers’ coding process. The logs from the development sessions have been analyzed using the fuzzy miner to highlight emergent and interesting developers’ and students’ behaviors during coding. The mined processes show different IDE usage patterns for students with different skills and performances. To validate our approach, we describe the results of a study in which the CodingMiner environment is used to investigate the coding activities of twenty students of a CS2 course performing a given programming task during four assignments. Results also demonstrate that fuzzy-based process mining techniques can be effectively exploited to understand students and developers behavior during programming tasks providing useful insights to improve the way they code.
research_question: Como técnicas de process mining baseadas em lógica fuzzy podem ser aplicadas para entender e melhorar as habilidades de codificação de estudantes e desenvolvedores através da análise de logs de IDE?
main_finding: A abordagem CodingMiner com técnicas de fuzzy-based process mining permite extrair padrões de uso de IDE e comportamentos emergentes de desenvolvedores e estudantes durante atividades de codificação. Os processos minerados revelaram diferentes padrões de uso de IDE para estudantes com diferentes níveis de habilidade e desempenho, fornecendo insights úteis para melhorar o processo de codificação.
limitations: Não mencionado explicitamente no resumo.
